title: CHA LOI CAVE – WHERE AND HOW TO DISCOVER IT FROM PHONG NHA?
description: Hidden in the rugged landscapes of Vietnam’s Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park lies a treasure waiting to be discovered – Cha Loi Cave. Escape the bustling tourist trails, this...
content:
Hidden in the rugged landscapes of Vietnam’s Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park lies a treasure waiting to be discovered – Cha Loi Cave. Escape the bustling tourist trails, this enchanting cave offers a journey into the heart of nature’s wonders. If you’re an adventurous soul seeking to explore off-the-beaten-path destinations, then Cha Loi Cave is your ticket to an unforgettable experience. 

Explore the hidden beauty inside Cha Loi Cave

The Mystique of Cha Loi Cave 

Cha Loi Cave is also known by names such as General Cave or Mr. Giap Cave. The reason for this name is because this was once the place where General Vo Nguyen Giap and Vietnamese soldiers stopped to rest while maintaining a strong resistance war against the Americans.  

Inside the cave, there is a natural formation resembling a statue, with its face turned inward, evoking thoughts of a national hero. It’s as if to commemorate and express gratitude to the elder brother of the People’s Army, hence the cave’s name, “Mr. Giap“.

The stone block is shaped like a General

This place where the Vietnamese soldiers came here to discuss work matters or relax after long and exhausting journeys. No matter how tense the war was, these heroic soldiers still organized cultural activities inside the cave, played musical instruments and sang. It seems like Cha Loi Cave is a historical witness, a close companion intertwined with the national revolutionary movement. 

Where is Cha Loi Cave?

Cha Loi Cave is situated in Quang Binh province, Vietnam, located next to Phong Nha – Ke Bang National Park, approximately 40 kilometers southwest of Dong Hoi City. This is a system of large caves located inside the limestone mountains. Cha Loi Cave has a simple and rustic appearance, but inside it contains the majestic and endless beauty of nature. This place possesses a system of stalactites and stalagmites with all shapes and unique geological layers as an impressive natural art. The special is that each year, this stalactite layer can grow about 0.03 mm longer. And note that you absolutely must not touch them.

Cha Loi Cave: A Hidden Gem

Cha Loi Cave, though not as renowned as some of its neighboring counterparts, boasts its distinctive charm. The cave’s allure lies in its natural grandeur. As you step into its depths, you are greeted by awe-inspiring rock formations, stalactites hanging delicately from the ceiling, and stalagmites rising majestically from the cave floor. Each chamber within Cha Loi Cave unveils a new tableau of geological artistry, showcasing the beauty sculpted by time and nature. Immerse yourself in the tranquil atmosphere as you wander through caverns adorned with awe-inspiring limestone formations and crystal-clear pools, feeling worlds away from the hustle and bustle of modern life.

A stunningly beautiful wonder inside the cave

This cave has a remarkable three-level structure, with a width ranging from 30 to 100 meters and an average height of 60 meters. The second level serves as the entrance, from the cave entrance you will move down to the first level and then climb to the exit on the third level. Even standing at the entrance, you can feel the cool breeze emanating from within the cave. Flowing beneath the cave is a year-round cool and clear underground river, along with small ponds that are home to various species of tiny shrimp and fish, creating a peaceful scene. The stalactites in the cave contain a lot of silica, so whenever light is shone on them, they become sparkling and stunningly beautiful.

The cool river flows inside the cave

Ecological Riches

Beyond its geological wonders, Cha Loi Cave harbors a delicate ecosystem. Bats, stalactite-dwelling insects, and unique cave-adapted flora contribute to the ecological richness of this subterranean haven. This cave is always moving and changing. Maybe next time we come back we’ll see something different.

The space inside the cave high and vast

Conquer Cha Loi Cave

To reach Cha Loi Cave, visitors must embark on an 800-meter trek along a winding trail that weaves through lush gardens and past the homes of the Bru-Van Kieu ethnic minority. This scenic journey offers a unique opportunity to immerse oneself in the local culture and witness the daily lives of the indigenous people before embarking on the cave exploration adventure.

When you pass the 800m journey, you will see a fairly small cave entrance, only about 4m2, appear before your eyes. When we step inside, a whole new world opens up, allowing us to discover new things, a magnificent wonder that cannot be found anywhere else. With huge blocks of stalactites, when light shines on these stalactites, it is as if they are given more energy, emits an extremely magical, shimmering light.

Huge stalactites

Cha Loi Cave boasts a unique “tunnel of love“, a pathway shaped like a heart formed by wind, water, and stalactites. Under the glow of lights, the stalactites gleam as if adorned with radiant gold, transforming the cave into an extraordinary, dazzling spectacle. This expansive tunnel is intertwined with tales of romantic love, and rumors suggest that those without a partner can come here to pray. 

The journey to explore Cha Loi cave will take you to many different levels of emotions, from excited anticipation and joy to overwhelm, amazement and finally remaining with indescribable joy when conquering this wonderful wonder.

Tunnel of love is extremely unique and splendid

Planning Your Exploration

The journey to explore Cha Loi Cave will begin from Phong Nha, a quaint town serving as the gateway to numerous natural wonders. Arrange transportation from Phong Nha to the vicinity of Cha Loi Cave. Options include guided tours or renting a motorbike for an independent adventure. The journey typically takes around 1-2 hours depending on your mode of transport. Prepare for a trek through lush forests and rugged terrain to reach Cha Loi Cave. Consider taking a guided tour to better understand the geology and historical significance of the cave. And remember to respect the fragile ecosystem of the cave and leave no trace behind.

The best time to explore Cha Loi Cave is during the dry season, which typically runs from April to March. During these months, the weather is more stable, with less rainfall, making it easier and safer to navigate through the surrounding terrain to reach the cave, and this is also a very suitable time for trekking. 

Exciting activities not to miss when exploring Cha Loi Cave
Trekking

To reach the cave, you’ll need to hike a 10km stretch, crossing through pristine forests and navigating the deep rocky ravines of the Coi Cave Gorge. What’s more, you’ll also need to swim through an underground river with temperatures ranging from 17 to 20 degrees Celsius. For adventure enthusiasts keen on conquering nature, this is undeniably an ideal destination not to be missed. It’s important to note that for a smooth exploration journey, it’s advisable to ensure good physical fitness, plan ahead, and heed the guidance of your tour guide to avoid getting lost.

Conquer rugged and dangerous cliffs

Camping at the Valley of Love

One of the beloved experiences when exploring Cha Loi Cave is camping at the Valley of Love in Coi village. A vast space surrounded by mountains and streams, it’s sure to be an incredibly exciting experience, offering moments of tranquility to dispel every day worries. Don’t forget to explore and engage with the unique culture and delicious cuisine of the Van Kieu ethnic minority living right here.

Immerse yourself in the vibrant campfire atmosphere at the Valley of Love

Tips for a smooth and enjoyable journey
Plan ahead: Research the route, weather conditions, and any necessary permits or reservations beforehand to avoid any surprises.
Pack appropriately: Bring essential items such as water, snacks, sturdy footwear, insect repellent, and a first aid kit. Bringing a flashlight or headlamp for exploring the cave.
Stay hydrated and energized: Keep yourself hydrated during the trek and refuel with nutritious snacks to maintain energy levels.
Follow safety guidelines: Listen to your tour guide’s instructions and stay on designated paths to ensure your safety. Be cautious when navigating uneven terrain and crossing water bodies.
Respect the environment: Preserve the natural beauty of the area by disposing of waste properly and refraining from damaging vegetation or wildlife.

Cha Loi Cave stands as a testament to the awe-inspiring beauty of Vietnam’s natural landscapes. From its hidden depths to its towering caverns, this enchanting cave invites intrepid explorers to embark on a journey of discovery. This promises to be an extremely interesting and exciting journey to conquer Cha Loi like never before. Pick up your backpack to start this special trip.

Are you ready to discover the enchantment of Cha Loi Cave? Your adventure awaits in the heart of Phong Nha!
author: VM Travel

title: Bái Đính Pagoda – A Spiritual & Cultural Masterpiece in Ninh Binh
description: Bái Đính Pagoda – A Spiritual & Cultural Masterpiece in Ninh Binh, Vietnam Nestled in the heart of Ninh Binh, Bái Đính Pagoda is a must-visit destination for travelers...
content:
Bái Đính Pagoda – A Spiritual & Cultural Masterpiece in Ninh Binh, Vietnam

Nestled in the heart of Ninh Binh, Bái Đính Pagoda is a must-visit destination for travelers seeking serenity and a deeper understanding of Vietnamese culture. Known as the largest Buddhist complex in Southeast Asia, Bái Đính offers breathtaking architecture, spiritual significance, and stunning natural surroundings.

Bai Dinh Pagoda

Why Visit Bái Đính Pagoda?
The Largest Buddhist Complex in Southeast Asia. Covering 700 hectares, Bái Đính Pagoda is a stunning fusion of ancient and modern Vietnamese architecture. The complex includes temples, towers, statues, and serene gardens, making it one of Vietnam’s most significant cultural and spiritual landmarks.
The Giant Buddha Statue. Witness the 10-meter tall, 100-ton bronze Buddha statue, the largest of its kind in Vietnam. It symbolizes peace and harmony, drawing pilgrims and visitors alike.
The Stupa of Bái Đính. Climb to the top of the 100-meter-tall stupa, housing sacred Buddha relics from India. From here, enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and karst mountains.
The Arhat Corridor. This 3-kilometer pathway is lined with 500 intricately carved Arhat statues, each representing a unique expression and pose. It is perfect for peaceful strolls and photography.
The Ancient Bái Đính Pagoda. Visit the original pagoda, located in a cave on the mountainside. With over 1,000 years of history, this sacred site offers a mystical experience and a connection to Vietnam’s ancient Buddhist traditions.
The Bell Tower. Admire the 36-ton bronze bell, whose soothing chimes echo through the pagoda, enhancing its tranquil atmosphere.
Things to Do at Bái Đính Pagoda
Join a Guided Tour. Learn about the pagoda’s history, significance, and symbolism from professional guides.
Take an Electric Cart Ride. Save time and energy by using electric carts to explore the vast complex. Price: 30,000 VND/person (~1.5 USD).
Attend Buddhist Ceremonies. Participate in prayers and rituals to immerse yourself in the local spiritual culture.
Enjoy Night Visits. Experience the magic of Bái Đính at night when the entire complex is beautifully illuminated, creating a serene and enchanting atmosphere.
Join in the 1-day Tour to visit Bai Dinh from Ha Noi.

Where to Eat Near Bái Đính Pagoda
Hoang Giang Restaurant. Specialties: Goat hotpot, crispy rice, and fresh fish from the local rivers. Distance: 10 minutes from the pagoda.
Bái Đính Vegetarian Restaurant. Specialties: Fresh and healthy vegetarian dishes inspired by Buddhist cuisine.. Location: Within the pagoda complex.
Tam Coc Cuisine. Specialties: Authentic Vietnamese dishes such as grilled goat, fish salad, and stir-fried vegetables.
Where to Stay Near Bái Đính
Bái Đính Garden Resort & Spa. Features: Tranquil villas surrounded by lush gardens, a spa, and a pool. Price: From 80 USD/night.
Ninh Binh Hidden Charm Hotel. Features: Modern rooms, a swimming pool, and close proximity to Bái Đính and Tràng An. Price: From 120 USD/night.
Tam Coc Rice Fields Resort features Rustic yet cozy accommodations with spectacular views of rice paddies. Prices start at 40 USD/night.
Suggested Itinerary for a Half-Day Visit

Morning:
• Arrive at Bái Đính Pagoda by 8:00 AM.
• Explore the Bell Tower, Arhat Corridor, and the Great Buddha Statue.
Mid-Morning:
• Visit the Ancient Bái Đính Pagoda and climb the Stupa for breathtaking views.
Lunch:
• Enjoy goat hotpot and crispy rice at Hoang Giang Restaurant.
Afternoon (Optional):
• Combine your visit with a boat tour at nearby Tràng An or Tam Coc. Consider here


Getting There

From Hanoi:
• By private car with driver: ~2 hours per way. Price here: Vietnam Car Rental with us
• By bus: 200,000 VND/person (~8 USD).
• Electric carts within the complex: 30,000 VND/person (~1.5 USD).

by VM Team

title: All you need about Car Rental in Vietnam 2025
description: Vietnam Car Rental in 2025 Renting a car in Vietnam is an excellent option for travelers looking to explore freely and reach destinations not accessible by public transportation. It’s...
content:
Vietnam Car Rental in 2025

Renting a car in Vietnam is an excellent option for travelers looking to explore freely and reach destinations not accessible by public transportation. It’s particularly useful for visiting cities like Ho Chi Minh City, Hue, and Dalat, which have attractions spread out across distances. Car rental offers flexibility, independence, and convenience, allowing you to create a custom itinerary. However, challenges like Vietnam’s notorious traffic and high rental costs are worth considering.

Self-driving in Vietnam is often difficult for foreigners due to traffic conditions and the licensing process.

Should You Rent With or Without a Driver?

Self-Drive Cars

Several agencies offer self-drive options, but foreign drivers need to convert their International Driving Permit into a Vietnamese license. Required documents include a valid passport, a driver’s license request form, a certified copy of your International Driving Permit, ID photos (3×4 size), and a fee.

However, due to complex traffic and the challenging license exchange process, most companies are reluctant to rent cars to those unfamiliar with local driving conditions. Popular rental companies in major cities include Da Nang Car Rental, Avis, VM Car, Green World Car Rental, Hue Private Car …

vietnam-car-rental

Car Rental with a Driver

Hiring a car with an English-speaking driver is often the best option for tourists in 2025. Local drivers know the routes well, making the journey more relaxed and worry-free. The key is to compare rates and choose a reputable agency. Upon booking, you’ll receive a detailed itinerary and a quote.

Check our professional chauffeur services here.

How to Rent a Car in Vietnam

Renting a car with a local driver in Vietnam is very easy. You’ll need to provide your information: name, phone number, pickup point and drop off point, date, and time. But it’s very difficult if you want to rent a self-drive car. Agencies may require an international license if you don’t have a Vietnamese one, and they may accept licenses in English or French. The agency will usually keep copies of these documents. After signing a rental agreement, you’ll typically pay a deposit ranging from 50-100% of the rental cost.

Most rental agencies are located in major cities such as Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City, where demand is high. Below are some reliable options for car rentals.

What Does Car Rental Cost in Vietnam?

Compared to other transport options, car rental in Vietnam is relatively costly. This is largely because public transport is affordable and widely available. Prices for rental cars vary from VND 500,000 to 10,000,000 per day (approximately $25-500), depending on the car model and luxury level. Common options include 4-seat and 7-seat vehicles. Fuel costs, around $1 per liter, are typically the customer’s responsibility, and payments are often made in cash. Some agencies may ask for a down payment or deposit.

So, for a short transfer, you can choose taxi or grab app. It’s cheaper.

But for a sightseeing transfer with a local driver, English-speaking driver, or city-to-city ride… You should choose private chauffeur services. , rental car full-day or by itinerary

Tips for Renting a Car in Vietnam

First, scrutinize the car’s condition, as many drivers often use rental vehicles and may not handle them with care. Visiting the agency’s showroom to find the right car is recommended.

Secondly, be mindful of Vietnam’s challenging traffic. The local driving environment can be intimidating even for experienced drivers due to the chaotic mix of motorbikes, honking, and congestion. If you’re not confident in navigating this, hiring a chauffeur is a wise option. Many agencies offer skilled local drivers who know the routes well, ensuring a safer and more enjoyable travel experience.

In Vietnam 2025, there are many services about Car rental that you can consider:

City-to-city rides, Your stress-free solution for long-distance rides with professional chauffeurs across the globe.
Chauffeur hailing, Enjoy the quality of a traditional chauffeur, with the convenience of riding within minutes of booking.
Airport transfers, With additional wait time and flight tracking in case of delays, our service is optimized to make every airport transfer a breeze.
Hourly and full day hire, For by-the-hour bookings or daily chauffeur hire, choose one of our tailored services for total flexibility, reliability and comfort.

title: Opening of the Visitor Center at Chan May Port
description: Grand Opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port The Visitor Center at Chan May Port has officially opened to support travelers arriving by cruise...
content:
Grand Opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port

The Visitor Center at Chan May Port has officially opened to support travelers arriving by cruise ship. This center offers valuable information and assistance for those interested in Vietnam Shore Excursions. Visitors can find guidance on local attractions, transportation options, and cultural insights, ensuring a memorable experience in the region. Whether you’re exploring nearby sites or planning your next adventure, the center is your essential resource for a fantastic visit.

Opening of the Visitor Center at Chan May Port

1. Where is Chan May Port?
2. Introduction to Chan May Port and Its Importance to Tourists Visiting Hue
3. Purpose of the Grand Opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port
4. How Far is Chan May Port from Da Nang?
5. Distance to Chan May Port from Hue?
6. How Far is Chan May Port from Hoi An?
7. Day Tours for Visitors from Chan May Port
7.1 Explore Hue Imperial City from Chan May Port
7.2 Join Hoi An City Tour
7.3 Discover Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge Tour
7.4 Da Nang City Tour: Sightseeing and Shopping
1. Where is Chan May Port?

Chan May Port is located in Binh An Village, Loc Vinh Commune, Phu Loc District, Thua Thien Hue Province, along the central coast of Vietnam. This port is between the cities of Da Nang and Hue, making it a convenient entry point for cruise ships visiting the central region of Vietnam. Chan May Port is also close to famous attractions like the ancient town of Hoi An, the imperial city of Hue, and the scenic Hai Van Pass, which connects Hue to Da Nang. The port serves as a key transit point for tourists looking to explore the UNESCO World Heritage sites in Central Vietnam.

Chan May Port in Hue Vietnam

2. Introduction to Chan May Port and Its Importance to Tourists Visiting Hue

Chan May Port is a major multi-purpose deep-water port that plays a vital role in connecting the East-West Economic Corridor. This port is one of the destinations that is easily accessible and connected to the world’s modern seaport centers and ensures the conditions and technical facilities to welcome the only cruise ship in Thua Thien Hue province. It is recognized as a key tourist destination, chosen by the Asian Cruise Association as one of 46 quality marine ports. Through its strategic position, it enhances the connectivity for tourism in Hue, enabling easy access for cruise ships and travelers to central Vietnam, including historical and cultural sites. This connectivity supports the local economy by promoting tourism and enhancing trade.

The port’s importance to tourism in Hue cannot be overstated. It provides seamless access for international visitors eager to experience Vietnam’s unique blend of historical and natural beauty. Many visitors use Chan May as their gateway to Hue, where they can immerse themselves in Vietnam’s rich imperial history at the Hue Citadel, the Forbidden Purple City, and the elaborate royal tombs along the Perfume River. Chan May Port’s location also makes it convenient for tourists to explore the scenic countryside and engage in local cultural activities, enhancing the overall experience for those seeking both adventure and a taste of traditional Vietnam.

With its proximity to UNESCO World Heritage sites, efficient services for docking large vessels, and an increasing focus on cruise tourism, Chan May Port plays a pivotal role in opening the treasures of Central Vietnam to the world.

Introduction to Chan May Port

3. Purpose of the Grand Opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port

Thua Thien Hue Department of Tourism in coordination with Chan May Port Joint Stock Company held an opening ceremony on October 25, 2024, officially putting into operation the Tourist Information and Support Center at Chan May port area.

The Grand Opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port is dedicated to enhancing the travel experience for visitors arriving in Central Vietnam. This center aims to provide comprehensive information and support, promoting Chan May Port as a welcoming gateway to the region’s rich cultural and natural attractions. Here, tourism information will be provided to visitors in various forms, such as brochures, tourism publications, maps, travel guides, and multimedia content, including images and clips related to local tourist destinations. Visitors will also receive essential advice to enrich their experiences in exploring cultural heritage, community tourism, eco-tourism, spiritual sites, traditional crafts, shopping, wellness, and more.

Especially, this center serves as a support point for cruise ship tourists in cases where assistance or emergency handling is needed, such as lost identification documents, personal belongings, or encounters with negative situations (such as overcharging, theft, or inappropriate behavior) while onshore for sightseeing.

Additionally, it will foster regional tourism growth, support local businesses, and elevate Central Vietnam’s profile as an international travel destination.

Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port

4. How Far is Chan May Port from Da Nang?

Chan May Port is about 45 kilometers from Da Nang, typically a 1.5-hour drive by car. A convenient location near famous cultural and tourist sites such as Lang Co Bay, Hai Van Pass, and Ba Na Hills,… makes it an ideal entry point for exploring Central Vietnam’s cultural and natural sites.

5. Distance to Chan May Port from Hue?

Chan May Port is approximately 50 kilometers south of Hue. The drive typically takes around 1 to 1.5 hours, depending on traffic. The route connects the bustling port with the ancient capital of Vietnam, highlighting the transition from maritime trade to imperial heritage. Chan May serves as a convenient starting point for exploring Hue’s famous sites like the Citadel, royal tombs, and pagodas.

Looking for the perfect day trip from your cruise? A Hue shore excursion from Chan May port is the ultimate way to discover Vietnam’s royal past. Journey through the scenic countryside to explore Hue’s Imperial City and iconic pagodas, all wrapped up in a worry-free schedule timed perfectly with your ship’s departure.

6. How Far is Chan May Port from Hoi An?

Chan May Port is about 80 kilometers north of Hoi An. The drive typically takes around 2 to 2.5 hours. As a key port, it serves as a gateway for cruise ships and travelers arriving by sea, making it easier for tourists to access Hoi An and nearby attractions such as Hoi An Ancient Town, Marble Mountains, My Son Sanctuary, etc.

7. Day Tours for Visitors from Chan May Port

Here are some popular day tours for visitors arriving at Chan May Port, designed to showcase the highlights of Central Vietnam in a short timeframe.

7.1 Explore Hue Imperial City from Chan May Port

Discover the historic capital of Vietnam, Hue, renowned for its imperial heritage. Visit the Imperial City (UNESCO World Heritage Site) with the ancient citadel and Forbidden Purple City, Khai Dinh Tomb, Thien Mu Pagoda – a historic 7-story pagoda by the Perfume River and enjoy a variety of Hue traditional cuisine. Don’t miss this chance to experience the rich culture and heritage of Vietnam—click here to book your Hue City Tour from Chan May Port today!

Explore Hue Imperial City from Chan May Port

7.2 Join Hoi An City Tour

Explore the charming town of Hoi An, known for its well-preserved architecture and vibrant atmosphere. Walk through Hoi An Ancient Town (a UNESCO World Heritage Site). Visits to landmarks like Japanese Covered Bridge, Assembly Hall, Ancient Houses and Temples… Free time for shopping or exploring the local art galleries and handicraft shops, enjoy Hoi An’s cuisine with dishes full of traditional flavors such as Cao Lau, Mi Quang, Banh Mi,… Discover the enchanting charm of Hoi An with our Hoi An City Tour!

Hoi An City Tour from Chan May Port

7.3 Discover Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge Tour

This tour takes you to the mountaintop entertainment complex of Ba Na Hills with the iconic Golden Bridge. Take the cable car ride to the summit and admire the breathtaking views of the surrounding forests and mountains. Visit the Golden Bridge (the hand-held bridge) for stunning views and marvel at majestic sceneries. Join us for an unforgettable adventure on the Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge Tour!

Discover Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge

7.4 Da Nang City Tour: Sightseeing and Shopping

Discover Da Nang’s modern cityscape along with its cultural and natural highlights. Visit Marble Mountains with stunning views and caves. Explore Linh Ung Pagoda and the Lady Buddha statue on Son Tra Peninsula. Explore Han Market for souvenirs and sample street food.

Da Nang City Tour

The opening of the Visitor Information and Assistance Center at Chan May Port marks an important step in enhancing the experience for travelers. With support from the center, visitors can easily plan their excursions and explore the local culture. Take this opportunity to discover the beauty of Chan May and nearby attractions, with dedicated assistance from the center!

title: TREKK TO DISCOVER PHONG NHA ABANDONED VALLEY – HANG E – HANG VANG
description: TREKK TO DISCOVER PHONG NHA ABANDONED VALLEY – HANG E – HANG VANG Recognized twice by UNESCO as a world natural heritage with different criteria, Phong Nha – Ke...
content:
TREKK TO DISCOVER PHONG NHA ABANDONED VALLEY – HANG E – HANG VANG

Recognized twice by UNESCO as a world natural heritage with different criteria, Phong Nha – Ke Bang National Park is the pride of the people of Quang Binh in particular and of Vietnam in general. Experiencing many changes in geology and geomorphology; Phong Nha – Ke Bang has displayed impressive evidence of earth’s history. This place is extremely blessed by Mother Nature with extremely majestic and wonderful landscapes. Phong Nha – Ke Bang National Park contains many mysteries of creation. Each cave is a splendid picture, decorated with a system of sparkling stalactites. Abandoned Valley is one of its gorgeous pieces, what wonderful things await you there? 

Unveiling the mysteries

The name “Hang E” is derived from the local Vietnamese language, where “Hang” means “cave.” The “E” in “Hang E” is a designation given to the cave by the British Cave Research Association (BCRA) during their explorations in the Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park in the early 1990s. The BCRA used a letter-based system to name the caves they discovered, and “E” was the fifth cave they explored in the area. When first discovered, Hang E was named Nuoc Xanh cave because of its strange blue water color and hidden many thrilling elements. Up to now, the expedition team has not yet determined where that blue water originates from and why the water is so blue. They only know that the water from Hang E flows abundantly along Tien stream to Nuoc Lan cave and pours out leisurely Dark cave (Hang Toi). In addition, Hang E is proudly called by local people by the beautiful name Thuy Cung Cave.

 

Hang Vang is known for its strikingly beautiful stalactites and stalagmites, which are illuminated by natural sunlight that filters through the cave’s openings. The cave’s name, “Hang Vang,” translates to “Golden Cave,” which is derived from the golden hues that bathe the cave’s interior during certain times of the day.

About Hang E and Hang Vang

Hang E (Hang E) is a cave located in the Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park in Quang Binh Province, Vietnam. It is one of the largest and most impressive caves in the world and is part of a complex of over 300 caves and grottos in the area. 

Location and Access:

Hang E is situated in the Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its karst landscapes and extensive cave systems.

The cave is located approximately 35 kilometers northwest of the town of Dong Hoi, which is the capital of Quang Binh Province.

 

Features and Highlights:

Hang E is found to be 736m long and its passage is usually 10-15m and 5m high which inevitably ends in a sump.

Hang E is the feeder to Dark Cave (Hang Toi) and both caves take an extremely large amount of water in the wet season when Hang Phong Nha river sink can’t take the volume of water. 

The cave features stunning stalactites and stalagmites, as well as unique rock formations and underground rivers.

Hang E is also home to a diverse ecosystem, with rare species of flora and fauna that have adapted to the cave’s unique environment.

Hang Vang is one of the lesser-known but equally stunning caves in the Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park in Vietnam. It is famous for its karst landscapes, underground rivers, and extensive cave systems:

Location and Access:

Hang Vang is located in the Abandoned Valley, a remote area within the Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park.

Features and Highlights:

The cave is relatively small compared to other caves in the area, making it a more intimate and immersive experience for visitors.

The cave is also home to unique rock formations, underground rivers, and small pools.

Journey to discover Abandoned Valley

When talking about Quang Binh, about Phong Nha – Ke Bang, surely not many people are familiar with the place Chay River – Dark Cave (Hang Toi), where you can freely zipline, participate in water sports, and take mud baths in caves inside Dark cave (Hang Toi). But few people know that, after a length of more than 5.2km of this cave, leading to the back door of Dark cave (Hang Toi) opens up a wild and mysterious beauty.

To reach and explore this side of Hang Toi, you will join the journey to explore Sinh Ton valley, with a total trip length of more than 8 km, including 4 km of steep roads and 4 km along the stream connecting two places are two wild and breathtakingly beautiful caves: Hang E and Hang Vang.

Visitors will walk across the vast grass hills to reach Dau Rai ancient forest, cross the sky gate, go up Tien stream to explore the 500m depth of Hang Nuoc Lan, which is the back door of Hang Toi. Seen from a distance, Hang Nuoc Lan looks like the wine jar of ancient ancestors, it is surrounded by groves of trees growing from rocky mountains. In front of the cave entrance are large rocks covered with green moss, like a screen protecting the cave entrance from the rainy seasons. The gentle green color of the forest trees mixed with the limestone color of the cave entrance hides many discoveries.

A special feature of Hang Nuoc Lan is the high ceiling made of black limestone, but more surprisingly, the stalactites flowing down from the ceiling are pristine white. Beneath the underground stream there are interlaced and rugged rocks, attracting the group to explore further. Not only that, on the sandy beach in the cave, new stalactites are forming from water drops falling from the cave ceiling…

Next, visitors will go through the primeval forest to Abandoned valley surrounded by extremely majestic vertical limestone mountains.

Continuing the journey through the forest and wading streams through Abandoned valley, you will continue to explore more than 500m of the length of Hang Nuoc Lan (back door of Hang Toi) and the magical Hang E. These are the oldest caves of the Hang Toi system, a unique model of geological, geomorphological and cave formation in Phong Nha – Ke Bang.

Nature awaits you!

Abandoned Valley was previously also known as Thung Tre because there grew a lot of bamboo and bamboo trees, which helped people collect forest bamboo shoots. Covering an area of up to 2,000 hectares, the valley converges many types of terrain, from limestone mountains, flat land, caves to underground streams and quiet primeval forests. This place also possesses biological diversity, known as the kingdom of birds, among which there are many rare species recorded in Vietnam and the world’s red books such as blue pheasant, red rooster, and persimmon. Right in the middle of the valley is Hang E (Hang Thuy Cung) with strangely blue water. Unlike Hang Nuoc Lan, which has undulating, multi-shaped rocks, in Hang E, the water is gentler, more feminine with jade green color… The deeper inside, visitors can admire the view. The cave is like an underwater aquarium with beautiful colorful stalactites, mysterious lines and even a long ringing sound when people call each other…

In particular, to get to Hang Thuy Cung, you will have to swim through the underground river with water as green as jade, with a beautiful landscape like an aquarium. The deeper you go inside, you will be amazed by the magnificence and magic of the multi-shaped stalactites. At the same time, this is an opportunity for visitors to better understand the biodiversity values of the world natural heritage forests of Phong Nha – Ke Bang National Park.

Tips for Visitors

Make sure to hire a knowledgeable guide who is familiar with the area and can ensure your safety throughout the trek.
Pack light but bring essentials such as water, snacks, a first-aid kit, and a flashlight.
Wear sturdy hiking boots and comfortable clothing suitable for trekking.
Respect the environment and local communities by leaving no trace and following any rules or guidelines provided by your guide.

The trip is a good opportunity to satisfy nature lovers and adventure enthusiasts. It not only meets mental requirements but also improves health. If you are wondering about those requirements for your trip, we – VM Travel will accompany and support you.

title: Top 4 Best Ways To Travel from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang City
description: Travel Danang from Ho Chi Minh city From South to Central Vietnam – The Ultimate Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang Guide! Da Nang an ideal tourist destination for...
content:
Travel Danang from Ho Chi Minh city

From South to Central Vietnam – The Ultimate Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang Guide!

Da Nang an ideal tourist destination for every trip. Da Nang is a coastal city in Central Vietnam, known as “the most livable city in Vietnam”. This city possesses many famous landscapes, its stunning beaches, captivating natural beauty, and vibrant culture; attracting domestic and foreign tourists.

Travel Da Nang from Ho Chi Minh city

1. Where is Da Nang in Vietnam
2. Danang to Ho Chi Minh Distance
3. How to get to Da Nang from Ho Chi Minh?
3.1 Flights from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang
3.2 Ho Chi Minh city to Da Nang Train
3.3 Bus from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang
3.4 Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang by Car
4. Suggested idea 3-days itinerary in Da Nang
4.1 Join tour Hoi An day trip from Da Nang
4.2 Explore Hue Imperial Citadel
4.3 Bana Hills and Da Nang city tour
4.4: Ba Thien Hau Pagoda
5. Is Da Nang Worth Visiting?
1. Where is Da Nang in Vietnam

Where is Da Nang in Vietnam? Located in the heart of Central Vietnam, Da Nang acts as a gateway to some of the country’s most popular tourist destinations. Its proximity to the East Sea provides easy access to stunning beaches, while its central position makes it a convenient base for exploring surrounding historical and cultural treasures like Hoi An, Hue, and My Son Sanctuary.

Da Nang in Vietnam

2. Danang to Ho Chi Minh Distance

Ho Chi Minh City to Da Nang distance according to main routes is as follows:

National Highway 14: 839 kilometers.
National Highway 1A and National Highway 20: 962 kilometers.
3. How to get to Da Nang from Ho Chi Minh?

To choose the type of vehicle depending on your preferences and financial ability to visit Da Nang and travel to tourist destinations, you can visit How to get from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang for more information.

3.1 Flights from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang

Airplane is the most convenient and fastest means of travel from Ho Chi Minh City to Da Nang. Travel time by plane is only about 1 hour and 40 minutes, much faster than by road.
Currently, all 4 domestic airlines are operating flights to Da Nang from Ho Chi Minh City. Flight frequency is quite dense, with about 20 flights/day. Ticket prices range from 36,000 – 1,829,000 VND/trip ( no taxes or fees).

Flights from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang

3.2 Ho Chi Minh city to Da Nang by Train

The distance from Ho Chi Minh City to Da Nang by rail is 950 kilometers. Every day there will be about 6 North- South trains departing from Saigon station and stopping at Da Nang station. Train ticket prices range from 510,000 – 1,100,000 VND/trip, depending on seat type and class. Travel time is from 15 to 19 hours, depending on the type of train and travel speed.

Ho Chi Minh city to Da Nang Train

3.3 Bus from Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang

Buses are a preferred choice for many tourists when traveling from Ho Chi Minh City to Da Nang. Because buses have reasonable ticket prices, travel time is relatively stable and there are many departure times to choose from.
Currently, there are many bus companies operating the Saigon to Da Nang route. You can refer to some reputable bus companies such as: Phuong Trang, Mai Linh, Hoang Long, etc.
Bus ticket prices from Ho Chi Minh City to Da Nang range from 430,000 VND/trip to 900,000 VND/turn, depending on seat type and bus company. Travel time is usually about 17 hours and 46 minutes.

Bus from Ho Chi Minh to Danang City

3.4 Ho Chi Minh to Da Nang by Car

Traveling to Da Nang by car is an interesting and attractive choice. Cars bring you comfort, freedom, and safety throughout the journey. You can be proactive in your schedule and stop and rest wherever you want. Besides, cars are a safer means of transportation than motorbikes, especially in harsh weather conditions.
Regarding the disadvantages, the cost of renting a self-driving car is higher than riding a motorbike or bus. However, if you go with a group of friends or family, this cost will be divided, helping you save a decent amount of money.

Ho Chi Minh to Danang by Car with English speaking Driver

4. Suggested idea 3-day itinerary in Da Nang

Da Nang is a famous tourist city with many landscapes, historical sites, rich cuisine and friendly people. A 3-day trip to Da Nang will bring you wonderful and unforgettable experiences.

4.1 Join tour Hoi An day trip from Da Nang

A day tour to Hoi An From Da Nang is a great choice for tourists who want to explore Hoi An Ancient Town without much time.
This tour usually departs from Da Nang early in the morning and ends in the evening. During this time, you will visit famous places of Hoi An ancient town such as:

Covered Bridge: This is the symbol of Hoi An ancient town. Covered Bridge was built during the Tran Dynasty and is a unique architectural work with a curved bridge design spanning a small creek.
Tan Ky Ancient House: This is an ancient house built during the Nguyen Dynasty, with typical architecture of Hoi An ancient town
Hoi An Silk Village: This is where Hoi An’s traditional silk is produced and traded. You can visit the silk production process and buy souvenirs made from silk
Hoi An Night Market: This is where you can enjoy Hoi An’s specialty dishes and shop for souvenirs.

In particular, you will be able to visit Hoi An ancient town at night, when lanterns are lit, creating a sparkling, magical scene. This is a great time for you to explore the beauty of Hoi An ancient town and immerse themselves in the bustling, vibrant atmosphere of the city.

Hoi An day trip from Da Nang

4.2 Explore Hue Imperial Citadel

The Imperial Citadel of Hue is a famous historical architectural work in Vietnam. This project was started in 1804 under the reign of King Gia Long and completed in 1833 under the reign of King Minh Mang.
Hue Imperial Citadel has a total area of about 500 hectares, including palaces, mausoleums, shrines, etc. This is the living and working place of the Nguyen Dynasty’s kings, as well as the place where political events take place, important culture and society of the court.
Hue Imperial Citadel was built according to traditional Vietnamese royal architecture, combined with some Western architectural elements. This project includes 10 city gates, 30 palaces, 22 shrines, etc.
The Imperial Citadel of Hue is an important historical site of Vietnam, recognized by UNESCO as a World Cultural Heritage. Join Hue City tour from Da Nang now!

Explore Hue Imperial Citadel

4.3 Bana Hills and Da Nang city tour

Ba Na Hills is an attractive destination not to be missed on a 3-day trip to Da Nang. From Da Nang city center to Ba Na Hills it takes about 1 hour by car or taxi. You can buy cable car tickets directly at Ba Na Hills cable car station or book tickets online in advance to save time.
The Ba Na Hills cable car line has a total length of 5,771.61 meters, recognized as the longest single-wire cable car line in the world. This cable car route has 3 routes, with a total travel time of about 20 minutes.
When riding the Ba Na Hills cable car, you will get a panoramic view of Da Nang city and majestic mountains and forests from above. This is an extremely interesting and memorable experience.
Here, you can visit famous places such as French Village, Golden Bridge, Wine Cellar, Linh Ung Pagoda, Love Flower Garden, etc. You can also participate in entertainment activities at Ba Na Hills like thrilling games, visiting the zoo, etc.
You can rent a taxi to move from Ba Na Hills to Da Nang city center. Travel time is about 1 hour. After arriving in the city center, you can walk or rent a motorbike to visit famous places such as Love Bridge, Dragon Carp Statue and Dragon Bridge. These are symbols of Da Nang city. Admiring the beauty of these works at night will bring you extremely interesting and memorable experiences. Join a day tour Bana Hills from Danang – Deluxe group tour now!

Bana Hills in Danang Vientam

5. Is Da Nang Worth Visiting?

In recent years, Da Nang has welcomed a large number of tourists to visit. This city possesses many famous landscapes, rich cuisine, and friendly, hospitable people.

Many attractive tourist attractions: Da Nang is blessed with many beautiful natural landscapes, from romantic beaches, and majestic mountains to mysterious caves such as: My Khe Beach, Ba Na Hills, Han River, etc.
Collection of many cheap hotels, resorts, etc, with beautiful views: Da Nang is a famous tourist city with a diverse accommodation system, meeting the needs of all types of tourists. In Da Nang, there are many hotels, motels, homestays, and resorts with many different prices, from affordable to high-end. Hotels in Da Nang are fully equipped with amenities, from modern rooms to convenient services such as swimming pools, spas, restaurants, etc.
Diverse cuisine, and cheap prices: Da Nang is a city famous for its rich and diverse cuisine. Dishes in Da Nang are made from fresh ingredients, with bold flavors of the Central region. Some famous specialties of Da Nang include My Quang, Cao Lau, Pork rice paper rolls, Fresh seafood, etc. Dishes in Da Nang are quite cheap, only from 15,000 VND to 50,000 VND/ dish. This is a big plus point that attracts tourists to Da Nang
The people are friendly and nice: Da Nang people often have bright smiles and gentle, sincere voices. They are always ready to help tourists, guide tourists on the way, and show tourists famous places to visit and eat.

If you are looking for an ideal tourist destination, Da Nang is a great choice for you. Have a wonderful time on your trip!

title: Best Time To Visit Ho Chi Minh City – Crafting Your Perfect Itinerary
description: Best Time to Visit Ho Chi Minh – Your Ultimate Guide to Timing the Perfect Visit Ho Chi Minh City is a tourist destination chosen by many tourists, both...
content:
Best Time to Visit Ho Chi Minh – Your Ultimate Guide to Timing the Perfect Visit

Ho Chi Minh City is a tourist destination chosen by many tourists, both domestic and international tourists. This city is located in the South and considered the most developed economic center in Vietnam. Besides, the city is also a convergence of many cultural and culinary elites. These things have made Ho Chi Minh city famous and increasingly attracting a large number of visitors. Visiting Ho Chi Minh City is something that you should not miss if you want to enjoy a memorable vacation. The tropical monsoon climate with two distinct dry and rainy seasons makes the atmosphere and beauty of the city much different from many other cities.

To have the smoothest journey to visit Ho Chi Minh, follow VM travel to know when is the best time to visit Ho Chi Minh City!

Best time to visit Ho Chi Minh City

1. Ho Chi Minh weather by Month
2. When to visit Ho Chi Minh city?
3. What to Pack and what to See?
3.1: Amusement Park
3.2: Amusement Park
4. Festival & event
4.1 Lunar New Year celebration
4.2: Mid-Autumn Festival
4.3: Nguyen Hue Flower Street Festival
4.4: Ba Thien Hau Pagoda
4.5: Whale Festival
5. What to do in Ho Chi Minh city?
5.1: Independence Palace
5.2: War Remnants Museum
5.3: Cu chi Tunnels
5.4: Ben Thanh Market
5.5: Bui Vien Street
6: How many day in Ho Chi Minh city?
1. Ho Chi Minh weather by Month

Ho Chi Minh weather by month is characterized by a sub-equatorial tropical climate with two rainy and dry seasons. The rainy season lasts from May to November and the dry season lasts from December to April. Below is a table of the weather and Ho Chi Minh city temperature by month and entertainment activities.

Based on the table above, you can clearly understand the weather and activities each month in Ho Chi Minh City to choose the best time to visit Ho Chi Minh City for a wonderful trip.

Ho Chi Minh weather by Month

2. When to visit Ho Chi Minh city?

Ho Chi Minh City in both the rainy and dry seasons has its own beauty and interesting activities. You can visit this place at any time of the year depending on your interests and schedule.

The best time to visit Ho Chi Minh city recommended in this article is during the dry season lasting from December to April. During the dry season Ho Chi Minh city temperature by month is very suitable for you to take a vacation, ranging from 22°C to 34°C. This is also the time when domestic tourism is most vibrant. In these months, the weather is quite warm and has little rain, which is very favorable for sightseeing and participating in many special activities, especially outdoor activities. The best month to visit Ho Chi Minh city is February because this is the month when Vietnamese people celebrate Lunar New Year.

Besides, Ho Chi Minh City is also known as “the city that never sleeps” when night activities are always flurrying. If you like to experience night parties, visiting Ho Chi Minh City in the rainy season months is also not a bad idea. Although at this time the weather is a bit erratic and not suitable for outdoor activities, it is an ideal time for you to gather with relatives and friends with many parties

Whether in the rainy or dry season, Ho Chi Minh City still has activities for you to participate in. It depends on what activities you want to participate in

When to visit Ho Chi Minh

3. What to Pack and what to See?
3.1 Rainy season

The rainy season in Ho Chi Minh city usually starts in May and ends in NovemberThis is when the weather is not very good because there is often heavy rain and there can even be tides and flooding. Therefore, if you travel to Ho Chi Minh City at this time, you should refer to the information below to know what items to bring and what to do in Ho Chi Minh City in this season

What to pack:
Raincoat: An indispensable item to help you avoid sudden heavy rains. Besides, equip an extra umbrella, wellington boots, and a waterproof backpack to ensure your body does not get wet to avoid the flu
UV protection items: Even though it’s the rainy season, there will still be sunny days or sunny mornings and rainy afternoons, so you should equip sun protection jackets, gloves, masks and sunscreen spray.
Attire: Light, waterproof clothing
If you want to camp in the primeval forests, you can bring your own tent or rent one. Mosquito spray is also essential for this activity
What to see:
Can Gio mangrove forest: Immerse yourself in the wild beauty of the mangrove forest and see rare animals.
Independence Palace: Learn about the ups and downs of Vietnam’s revolutionary milestones
Enjoy tropical fruits: Participate in the Southern fruit festival held in Suoi Tien starting from June to the end of August every year
Mid-Autumn Festival: See lanterns sold on the streets and enjoy mooncakes – a cultural feature of Vietnamese people.

Ho Chi Minh at night

3.2 Dry season

The dry season starts in December and ends at the end of April. In stark contrast to the rainy season, the dry season is the best time to visit Ho Chi Minh city.

What to pack:
Clothing: In general, Ho Chi Minh’s weather is quite hot, with extremely hot months, clothes with good sun protection and effective sweat absorption will be the best choice
UV protection equipment: UV rays in Ho Chi Minh City are very harmful to the skin so you need to equip hats, glasses, masks, gloves, socks and sun protection shirts. Sunscreen is definitely something you cannot do without
Shoes will be the optimal choice that can provide both sun protection and convenience for walking
Drinks: Dehydration is unavoidable in hot weather, so having filtered water with you is a necessity.
What to see:
Bui Vien Walking Street: Experience the busiest street for young people in Ho Chi Minh City with many entertainment activities
Flower Street on Nguyen Hue Street: Watch the flowers bloom and take photos to preserve the last days of the year
Visit Ba Thien Hau Pagoda: Explore the ancient temple with unique architecture and valuable cultural products
Visit Notre Dame Cathedral: Visit the most prominent Catholic architectural work – the symbol of Ho Chi Minh City

Both seasons in Ho Chi Minh City will bring you different experiences and activities but still ensure the excitement of each activity for you.

Ho Chi Minh city tour

4. Festival & event

From tasting street food at local markets to immersing yourself in the youthful atmosphere on vibrant walking streets filled with entertainment activities.

4.1 Lunar New Year celebration

Lunar New Year in Ho Chi Minh City is celebrated on January 1 according to the Eastern lunar calendar. This is a traditional festival with a long history. In addition, this is also considered a major holiday of the year for all Vietnamese people. At this time, under the warm golden sunlight, the streets of Ho Chi Minh City will be decorated with fresh flowers and signs with wishes in bright red colors of luck. On the street, people are more crowded than ever at the flower market to prepare for a brilliant new year. If you have the opportunity to come to Ho Chi Minh City during this time, you will witness a city full of colorful flowers and participate in traditional festivals during Vietnamese New Year.

Below are some suggestions on places and activities you can participate in during Tet in Ho Chi Minh City.

Places to visit:
Ho Thi Ky Flower Market
Nguyen Hue Tet Flower Street
Notre Dame Cathedral
Ong Do Street
Landmark Observatory
Ben Thanh market
Activities:
Watch Water Puppet show at Phuong Nam Art Theater
Join Amazing Bay water park
Go to the temple to pick fortune
SUP on the Saigon River
Enjoy the beautiful streets of Ho Chi Minh City on a double-decker bus
Takes photos at Maison de Boony cafe
Some Dishes you should try
Chung cake, Tet cake
Jellied meat
Lean pork pie
Dried candied fruit

This is truly a golden time for you to choose to visit Ho Chi Minh City because of the warm, cool, sunny weather. Moreover, Lunar New Year is a bustling festival with a variety of festivals, guaranteed not to disappoint you on this journey

Lunar New Year Celebration

4.2: Mid-Autumn Festival

Mid-Autumn Festival is also known as Children’s Festival because most children look forward to this day to carry lanterns and eat moon cakes. Ho Chi Minh City celebrates the Mid-Autumn Festival on the 15th day of the 8th lunar month. On this holiday, on every street you can easily see lanterns with many different designs and colors sold in stores. In addition, on this Tet holiday, there are also lion dance and dragon dance festivals often held on city center roads and walking streets such as Nguyen Hue street.

If you are lucky, you will see performances by professional lion dance groups. The atmosphere of this festival is very joyful and bustling. Here are some places you can visit

Luong Nhu Hoc lantern street
Starlight Bridge
City Children’s House
Suoi Tien Park, Dam Sen

Mid-Autumn Festival in Ho Chi Minh City

4.3: Nguyen Hue Flower Street Festival

Nguyen Hue is considered the most beautiful street in Ho Chi Minh City. The Nguyen Hue Flower Street Festival is usually held from December 28 to January 4 of the lunar calendar. This road will be splendidly decorated with many fresh flower pots and mascots creating splendor. Thanks to its brilliant beauty, Nguyen Hue Flower Street Festival not only attracts many domestic tourists but also foreign tourists to visit and take photos. Becoming a place where many people come to spring vacation every Tet holiday

Nguyen Hue Flower Street Festival

4.4: Ba Thien Hau Pagoda

This is a large temple located in District 5, the center of Cho Lon, Ho Chi Minh City. The temple was built around 1760 and has gone through many historical events but still stands tall to this day. Thanks to its ancient and quiet beauty, the temple has become a spiritual place that many tourists come to visit. Every year on the 14th day of the first lunar month, the pagoda will hold the Thien Hau pagoda fest
author: VM Travel

title: Exploring Halloween Holiday in Central Vietnam 2023
description: Halloween, a holiday deeply rooted in Western culture, has transcended borders and is now celebrated in various parts of the world. Central Vietnam, with its unique blend of traditions...
content:
Halloween, a holiday deeply rooted in Western culture, has transcended borders and is now celebrated in various parts of the world. Central Vietnam, with its unique blend of traditions and influences, has embraced this spooky holiday with open arms. While it may not have the same historical significance in Vietnam as it does in the United States or Europe, Halloween has taken on a life of its own in this charming region.

In this article, we will explore the intriguing and ever-evolving Halloween celebrations in Central Vietnam. From costume parties to deliciously haunted food and a mix of cultural elements, Halloween in this part of the world offers a truly unique and unforgettable experience.

Halloween in a Vietnamese Context
Embracing the Western Tradition

Halloween’s popularity in Vietnam can be attributed to the country’s growing openness to Western cultures and traditions. The younger generation, in particular, has enthusiastically embraced Halloween as a fun and exciting occasion. This cultural exchange has led to the rise of Halloween-themed events and celebrations throughout the country.

The Influence of Pop Culture

The influence of Western pop culture, including movies, TV shows, and social media, has played a significant role in introducing and popularizing Halloween in Vietnam. Costumes and decorations inspired by popular films and series are commonly seen during the holiday season.

Central Vietnam’s Halloween Celebrations
The Emergence of Themed Parties

In Central Vietnam, cities like Da Nang, Hoi An, and Hue have become hubs for Halloween celebrations. One of the most prominent ways Halloween is celebrated in the region is through themed parties. These parties often feature elaborate decorations, spooky costumes, and live music, creating a vibrant and festive atmosphere.

Mysterious Delicacies
Sticky Rice with Pumpkin (Xôi Bí Đỏ): Pumpkin is a staple of Halloween, and in Central Vietnam, it’s often used to make sticky rice. The combination of sweet pumpkin and glutinous rice creates a delightful treat that’s perfect for the season.
Grilled Blood Sausage (Huyết Đặc): Blood sausage might not be for the faint of heart, but it’s a popular dish during Halloween in Vietnam. It’s seasoned with herbs and spices, giving it a unique and bold flavor.
Crispy Fried Quails (Chim Cút Chiên Giòn): These crispy fried quails are a popular snack during Halloween celebrations. They are seasoned with a mix of spices and served with a dipping sauce, making them a mouthwatering indulgence.
Top Destinations for Halloween

Central Vietnam offers an array of exciting destinations to celebrate Halloween. Whether you’re a local or a tourist, here are some places to immerse yourself in the Halloween spirit:

Da Nang: The Modern Halloween Hub: Da Nang hosts numerous Halloween-themed events and parties. Ba Na Hills: to experience lively Halloween celebrations
Hoi An: Ancient Town, Spooky Delights: Hoi An’s picturesque streets come alive with Halloween decorations and themed events. Explore the historic town while enjoying the spooky atmosphere.
Hue: DMZ Bar in the walking street.

In these destinations, you can enjoy not only the local Halloween cuisine but also vibrant parties, haunted house attractions, and other exciting activities that make Central Vietnam a fantastic place to celebrate this spooky holiday.

As Halloween continues to grow in popularity in Central Vietnam, the region offers a one-of-a-kind fusion of Western and Vietnamese traditions, creating a memorable and unique experience for all who partake in the festivities. So, when October 31st rolls around, don your costume, explore the delicious Halloween-themed dishes, and immerse yourself in the enchanting atmosphere of this captivating holiday.

Get ready for a spooky yet culturally enriching experience as you explore the Halloween celebrations in Central Vietnam. Embrace the fusion of Western and Vietnamese traditions, savor deliciously haunted food, and partake in a variety of unique activities that make Halloween in this region truly special. Whether you’re a resident or a tourist, Central Vietnam offers a memorable Halloween experience like no other.

FAQs

1. Is Halloween widely celebrated in Vietnam?

Halloween has gained popularity, especially among the younger generation. While it may not be as widely celebrated as traditional Vietnamese holidays, you can expect to find Halloween events and festivities in major cities.

2. Can tourists participate in Halloween events in Central Vietnam?

Absolutely! Tourists are welcome to join Halloween celebrations in Central Vietnam. Many venues and businesses cater to tourists during this festive season, so you can enjoy the spooky fun as well.

3. What are some popular Halloween costumes in the region?

Popular costumes often draw inspiration from Western pop culture, including movie characters, ghosts, vampires, and zombies. Traditional Vietnamese outfits with a spooky twist are also common.

4. Are there any specific superstitions related to Halloween in Vietnam?

While Halloween is primarily a fun and festive occasion in Vietnam, some individuals may still observe traditional superstitions related to ghostly or supernatural elements. These beliefs can vary from region to region.

5. What are some alternative ways to enjoy Halloween in Central Vietnam?

Apart from the typical Halloween parties and events, consider exploring local night markets, indulging in themed street food, or even visiting haunted house attractions for a unique Halloween experience.

title: The Mid-Autumn Festival (Tet Trung Thu) of Vietnamese today
description: The Mid-Autumn Festival, known as “Tet Trung Thu” in Vietnam, is one of the most beloved and colorful festivals in Vietnamese culture. It typically falls on the 15th day...
content:
The Mid-Autumn Festival, known as “Tet Trung Thu” in Vietnam, is one of the most beloved and colorful festivals in Vietnamese culture. It typically falls on the 15th day of the 8th lunar month, which is the 29th of September this year, 2023. Have you ever hear the story of the Mid-Autumn Festival in Vietnam?
 THE LEGEND OF MR. CUOI 

In Vietnamese folklore, there’s a tale of Cuoi, who, in a fierce battle with a tiger, stumbled upon a miraculous plant capable of reviving the dead. Motivated by compassion, Cuoi cultivated this precious herb to heal his ailing wife and fellow villagers. However, one fateful day, his wife, in a moment of forgetfulness, inadvertently caused the magical tree and Cuoi himself to soar into the sky. Thus, Cuoi found himself on the moon, forever tending to the invaluable tree.

Since that time, every full moon, when the night sky is aglow with the moon’s radiant brilliance, people gazing skyward can spot a silhouette resembling an ancient tree, with someone seated beneath it. It’s believed to be none other than Mr. Cuoi, patiently awaiting the day he can return to Earth.

In the hearts of all, the full moon of August signifies the descent of Hang Nga and Cuoi to Earth for a joyous reunion. During the Mid-Autumn Festival, the young ones revel in vibrant activities like lion dances and lantern parades. Each family arranges an altar in their courtyard to pay respects, then gathers to marvel at the moon and share in a delightful feast together. This time-honored tradition embodies the enduring spirit of unity, celebration, and the magical allure of the Mid-Autumn Festival.

 THE LEGEND OF HANG NGA 

Hang Nga, also known as Ms. Hang, is a renowned figure in the rich tapestry of mythological tales spanning China, Vietnam, and Japan. According to legend, Hang Nga was described as a woman of captivating beauty, the beloved wife of Hau Nghe, a heroic figure renowned for his feat of shooting down nine suns to aid his people.

As the story goes, Hang Nga’s destiny took a celestial turn when she received the elixir of immortality from the Western Queen Mother. However, the elixir’s potency was so great that it propelled her into the heavens, where she found her new abode on the moon.

Ever since this enchanting tale unfolded, the tradition of moon-gazing during the Mid-Autumn Festival has endured through generations. It is believed that by gazing at the moon, people pay homage to the benevolent Hang Nga, beseeching her for good fortune and a tranquil existence.

This timeless legend serves as a testament to the enduring power of storytelling and the profound cultural significance of the Mid-Autumn Festival, where the moon becomes a symbol of hope, luck, and the eternal beauty of Hang Nga.

 THE LEGEND OF THE JADE RABBIT 
According to Eastern culture, from ancient times, there was a pair of rabbits who cultivated and attained enlightenment and came to meet the Jade Emperor. When they arrived at the Southern Heaven Gate, the rabbits suddenly saw Hang Nga being escorted to the moon by the Venus Owner. Touched by the story that to save the people, Hang Nga had to break the laws of heaven, the fairy rabbit sent the youngest rabbit to the moon to befriend her.
 TRADITIONS AND MOONCAKES 
Today, the Mid-Autumn Festival in Vietnam is a time for families to reunite, express their love for one another, and celebrate the harvest season. Children carry colorful lanterns and parade through the streets in a vibrant lantern procession. Mooncakes, which come in various flavors and designs, are exchanged and enjoyed as a symbol of unity.
The Mid-Autumn Festival is a magical and heartwarming celebration that highlights the importance of family, community, and selflessness. It continues to be a cherished tradition that brings people together to appreciate the beauty of the moon and the spirit of love and giving.
29/09/2023 – The Mid-Autumn Festival in Vietnam

title: VM Travel join the International Travel Expo Ho Chi Minh City 2023
description: VM Travel participated in Vietnam’s largest international travel expo, ITE 2023, held in Ho Chi Minh City over three days in September 2023. Let’s take a closer look at...
content:
VM Travel participated in Vietnam’s largest international travel expo, ITE 2023, held in Ho Chi Minh City over three days in September 2023. Let’s take a closer look at what was interesting about ITE 2023.

International travel expo, ITE 2023

The International Travel Expo (ITE) is Vietnam’s largest and most prestigious international tourism trade event. The 17th ITE 2023 was held in Ho Chi Minh City over the course of three days, from 7th to 9th September. ITE 2023 was organized at the Saigon Exhibition and Convention Center with the theme “Connectivity, Growth, Sustainability”. ITE has attracted over 400 exhibiting companies, 250 high-level buyers from over 40 countries and territories, and more than 25,000 visitors.

The event is expected to symbolize the resilience, strength, and creativity of Vietnam’s tourism industry during unprecedented turmoil and difficulty for the global tourism industry. ITE 2023 is to create a coordination and mutual support network platform between tourism associations and local tourism businesses across the country. It will promote the domestic tourism market and create synergy to improve the competitiveness of Vietnam Tourism on the world tourism map. ITE 2023 is also a flash point for Inbound tourism promotion that reopens after the COVID-19 pandemic.

One of the highlights of ITE – HCMC 2023 is the exceptional promotional activities, including introductions to tourism from Malaysia, Cambodia, Laos, Thailand, South Korea, and Japan. Visitors at the public consumption festival will also enjoy attractive incentives from travel companies, airlines, restaurants, and hotels. These incentives may include discounts on tour packages, affordable airline tickets, promotional offers, lucky draws, and more.

ITE – HCMC 2023

VM Travel is honored to accompany and participate in the 17th ITE 2023. It is a golden opportunity for us to meet and greet with potential partners and buyers worldwide. This event also allowed VM Travel to showcase our distinctive tourism programs and VM Travel-branded products to partners and visitors from various countries and continents, including India, Australia, Singapore, Qatar, the Philippines, Korea, China, and more.

With booth number 352 and the theme “Let us show you Central Vietnam”, the VM Team is proud to be one of the few travel agencies in Central Vietnam on hand at the event. Here, we provided partners, buyers, and visitors with our curated travel products via high-quality catalogs, brochures, and posters. With professional and thoughtful preparation, VM’s booth attracted many buyers and visitors to come and learn about the unique tour packages of VM Travel in Central Vietnam.

At ITE 2023, VM Travel engaged in meaningful conversations with businesses and customers. These discussions provided us with valuable insights from industry professionals, and we also shared local travel advice with our customers. We highly appreciated the opportunity to participate in ITE as it allowed us to introduce our travel services and bring Central Vietnam’s beauty closer to domestic and international friends.

ITE 2023 in Ho Chi Minh City

VM Travel had a memorable and successful ITE 2023 in Ho Chi Minh City. Taking part in the 17th International Travel Expo marks an essential milestone for VM to get closer to becoming a leading tour operator in Indochine countries and the world.

title: Bana Hills Cable Car
description: Bana Hills Cable Car: A Journey to Remember Are you looking for a unique way to experience the natural beauty of Vietnam? Look no further than the Ba Na...
content:
Bana Hills Cable Car: A Journey to Remember

Are you looking for a unique way to experience the natural beauty of Vietnam? Look no further than the Ba Na Hills Cable Car! This incredible attraction offers breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and forests, as well as access to a variety of exciting activities and attractions. In this article, we’ll explore everything you need to know about the Bana Hills Cable Car, from its history and engineering to the many adventures that await you on your visit.

1. History of the Bana Hills Cable Car
2. Design and Engineering
3. Riding the Cable Car
4. Attractions at Ba Na Hills
4.1: Amusement Park
4.2: Hiking and Camping
5. Tips for Visiting Bana Hills
5.1: Best Time to Visit
5.2: Weather Conditions
5.3: Tickets and Prices
5.4: Food and Beverages
1. History of the Bana Hills Cable Car

The Bana Hills Cable Car was first opened in 2013, and it quickly became one of the most popular tourist attractions in Vietnam. The cable car was designed and built by the world-renowned cable car manufacturer Doppelmayr, which is based in Austria. The project took three years to complete and cost an estimated $200 million.

The cable car was built to provide visitors with access to Ba Na Hills, a mountain resort that had previously been difficult to reach. French colonialists first established the resort in the early 20th century, and it quickly became a popular retreat for wealthy French families. However, the resort was largely abandoned during the Vietnam War, and it fell into disrepair in the years that followed.

In the 1990s, the Vietnamese government began investing in the redevelopment of Ba Na Hills, and the Bana Hills Cable Car was a key part of this effort. Today, Ba Na Hills is a thriving tourist destination that attracts visitors from around the world.

bana hills vietnam cable car

2. Design and Engineering

The Bana Hills Cable Car is a true engineering marvel. It stretches over 5,771 meters (18,930 feet) and is the longest cable car in the world. The cable car consists of 86 cabins, each of which can carry up to 10 passengers. The ride takes approximately 20 minutes, and it offers stunning views of the mountains and surrounding countryside.

The cable car was designed to withstand even the most extreme weather conditions, including typhoons and earthquakes. It is also equipped with state-of-the-art safety features, including an automatic braking system and a backup power supply.

bana hills cable car timetable

3. Riding the Cable Car

Riding the Bana Hills Cable Car is an unforgettable experience. As you ascend the mountain, you’ll be treated to panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. You’ll see lush forests, towering mountains, and sparkling waterfalls. It’s a great opportunity to take photos and enjoy the scenery.

Once you reach the top of the mountain, you’ll have access to a variety of exciting activities and attractions. You can explore the amusement park, which features a variety of rides and games. You can also visit the gardens and temples, which offer a glimpse into Vietnamese culture and history. And if you’re feeling adventurous, you can go hiking or camping in the surrounding mountains.

ba na hills cable car ticket

4. Attractions at Ba Na Hills

Ba Na Hills offers a wide range of attractions and activities for visitors of all ages. Here are some of the highlights:

4.1: Amusement Park

The amusement park at Ba Na Hills features a variety of rides and games, including a roller coaster, a Ferris wheel, and a haunted house. There are also arcade games, bumper cars, and other attractions for kids and adults alike.

Ba Na Hills is home to several beautiful gardens and temples, including the Linh Ung Pagoda. This stunning temple is located at the peak of the mountain and offers breathtaking views of the surrounding area. The temple is dedicated to the Buddhist goddess of mercy, and it features intricate carvings and colorful decorations.

In addition to the Linh Ung Pagoda, Ba Na Hills also has several other gardens and parks that are worth exploring. The Sun World Ba Na Hills Garden is one of the most popular. It features a variety of exotic flowers and plants, as well as stunning sculptures and statues.

Another popular attraction is the French Garden, which was designed to resemble a traditional French village. It features charming cottages, winding streets, and colorful gardens. It’s a great place to take a leisurely stroll and enjoy the peaceful surroundings.

ba na hills cable car length

4.2: Hiking and Camping

For those who love the great outdoors, Ba Na Hills offers plenty of opportunities for hiking and camping. There are several trails that wind through the surrounding mountains, offering stunning views of the landscape. You can also camp overnight in the mountains, either in a tent or in a cabin.

One of the most popular hiking trails is the Golden Bridge Trail. This trail leads to the famous Golden Bridge, a stunning pedestrian bridge that offers incredible views of the mountains and valleys below. The trail is relatively easy and takes about 30 minutes to complete.

Golden Bridge at Bana Hills

5. Tips for Visiting Bana Hills

If you’re planning a visit to Golden Bridge and Ba Na Hills, there are a few things you should keep in mind:

5.1: Best Time to Visit

The best time to visit Ba Na Hills is during the dry season, which runs from November to March. During this time, the weather is cool and dry, with temperatures ranging from 15 to 25 degrees Celsius. This makes it perfect for outdoor activities like hiking, camping, and exploring the gardens and temples.

In contrast, the rainy season runs from April to October and can be quite wet and humid, with frequent rainstorms and occasional typhoons. While the lush green landscape is beautiful during this time, it can make outdoor activities more challenging and less enjoyable.

ba na hills cable car

5.2: Weather Conditions

It’s important to note that the weather in Ba Na Hills can be unpredictable, with sudden changes in temperature and rainfall. Visitors should come prepared with appropriate clothing and gear for any weather conditions.

5.3: Tickets and Prices

Tickets and prices for Ba Na Hills can vary depending on the type of ticket and time of year. There are a variety of ticket options available, ranging from a basic admission ticket to a package deal that includes various attractions and activities.

As of 2024, the basic admission ticket for Ba Na Hills starts at 900,000 VND (approximately $40) for adults and 700,000 VND (approximately $31) for children. This includes access to the cable car, French village, and various gardens and temples.

There are also packages available that include additional attractions such as the wax museum, wine cellar, and amusement park. These packages range in price from 900,000 VND (approximately $39) to 2,100,000 VND (approximately $91) per person, depending on the package and time of year.

It’s important to note that prices may be higher during peak tourist seasons, such as holidays and weekends. Additionally, prices may be subject to change, so contact us for early booking.

5.4: Food and Beverages

Ba Na Hills offers a wide variety of food and beverage options for visitors to enjoy. There are several restaurants and cafes throughout the park, serving a range of cuisines from Vietnamese to European.

One of the most popular dining options at Ba Na Hills is the French Village, which features several cafes and restaurants that serve traditional French cuisine. The village also has a wine cellar that offers a variety of wines from around the world.

For those who prefer Vietnamese cuisine, there are several restaurants that serve local dishes such as pho, bun cha, and banh mi. Visitors can also enjoy street food-style snacks such as Banh Xeo (Vietnamese crepes) and grilled meat skewers.

In addition to restaurants, there are several cafes and bars throughout Ba Na Hills where visitors can enjoy a cup of coffee or a cocktail while taking in the views. Many of these cafes offer outdoor seating with beautiful views of the surrounding mountains and gardens.

It’s important to note that prices for food and beverages at Ba Na Hills can be higher than other places in Vietnam due to the park’s status as a tourist destination. However, there are still affordable options available for budget-conscious travelers.
