title: Hue Airport Vietnam
description: Hue Airport Vietnam is a well-know destination for flights in central Vietnam. People often call the local name as Phu Bai Airport. Although it’s not too huge and spacious...
content:
Hue Airport Vietnam is a well-know destination for flights in central Vietnam. People often call the local name as Phu Bai Airport. Although it’s not too huge and spacious an airport, Phu Bai International Airport still provides a wide range of essential services. The code of this airport is HUI , it’s easier to distinguish with other’s airports.

Hue airport Vietnam comprises two floors with total area of 6,539 m2 which means it can welcome over 1,5 million customers yearly. On the first floor, it is the designed spot for restricted arrivals, which consists international and domestic arrivals. Besides that, on the first floor, you also see the check-in security, lounge, food and drink corners, information center, airline offices, ATM for withdraw cash. On the second floor, it is a space for departure flights and it includes three terminals. Souvenir shops, restaurants and lounges are also located to serve passengers. Some airlines work at Phu Bai airport such as Vietnam airline, Vietjet, Bamboo airways and Jetstar Pacific.

Hue Airport Vietnam – All Information for Tourist
1: Where is Hue Airport Vietnam
2: How far is it
2.1: To Hue City Center
2.2: To Da Nang City
2.3: To Hoi An City
3: Hue Airport Vietnam Services
4: How to get from Hue Airport to Hue City Center
4.1: By Bus
4.2: By Car
4.3: By Taxi
1. Where is Hue Airport Vietnam

Hue Airport is located on 1A Highway, Phu Bai Ward, Huong Thuy Town, Thua Thien Hue Province, which is 15km to the southeast of Hue’s city center.

2. How far is it
2.1: To Hue City Center

Approximately, it will take around 30 minutes to reach the city center from Hue airport because of 15 km distance. You can easily use multiple transports such as taxi, motorbike, public bus or private car from a travel agent. Especially, booking private car is the most reliable way for transfer. After checking in your hotel in city center, you can start discovering Hue’s attractions. There are many things to do in this city, our driver can share you some tips or information when he drop you at the hotel.

You can book a car with Engish Speaking Driver via here: Hue Airport Transfer

2.2: To Da Nang City

Traveling to Danang city from Hue airport is convenient by private car or taxi .It is about 78,5km, the duration of travel is one hour and haft to reach the destination. If you don’t feel tired after the flight, on the way the chauffeur will drive you visit some attractions such as Lang Co beach, Lap An lagoon, Hai Van Pass, Elephant Springs, etc.

2.3: To Hoi An City

You also can travel to Hoi An from Hue airport, the distance is 112km. Roughly it will cost 2 hours and 30 minutes for transfer. You can embark a private car from a travel agent or taxi. Additionally, you also have an opportunity to sightseeing to amazing destinations such as Lap An Lagoon, Lang Co beach, Hai Van Pass, Marble Mountain, Dragon Bridge, My Khe beach, etc.

3. Hue Airport Vietnam Services

Food and Drink: Hue airport serves food and drink in both floors, you can choose different options from the menu of the restaurants. They serve local cuisines and fast foods. You must try the Bun Bo Hue – Hue Noodle Soup when you come to our ancient captital. The price of food and drink are higher cost but not overpriced.

Sleeping: It depend on the duration of your layover at Hue airport, you can select the place for resting. If you have a short break, you can sleep at the bench . If you feel tired and need to recharge the energy, you can look for some hotels, which are close by the airport. We recommend you stay in Senna Hue Hotel, Scarlett Boutique Hotel, Indochine Palace Hotel, Serene Hotel, Jade Hotel, Alba Spa Hotel, etc. It will just take around 30 minutes by car or bus to arrive the hotel.

Shopping: Duty-free isn’t applicable in Phu Bai airport. However, you can shop for clothes, local special foods, handicraft souvenirs such as accessories, jewelry in department stores on the ground and the first floor.
ATM / Bank Services: If you need cash to spend, there are several ATM machines at the first floor. Transaction fee is applied when you withdraw cash, there is a limited amount to get money from an ATM so you should notice about this too.

4: How to get to Hue City Center

You can choose different options to travel from Hue airport to Hue city center. Public bus, private car from a travel agent, taxi or motorbike are available transfer you. It is depend on your travel purpose; you can select the suitable vehicles for your trip.

4.1: By Bus

This is the cheapest option to travel from Hue airport to city center. If you don’t carry many suitcases, you can travel by bus. Roughly, it will take 30 – 40 minutes to arrive the city center. The price for bus transfer is 90,000 VND = 3 USD. The bus works from 8:20am to 10:45pm daily, it come every 30 minutes so you don’t have to wait for too long. You can purchase the ticket directly from the airport. It will drop you off at your hotel within the center. There are 10 to 25 buses works everyday. The phone contact is 1900 6763.

4.2: By Car

This is the most reliable option for passengers who wish to travel from Hue airport to city center. Before your travel date, you can book through online from a travel agent a private car, they will immediately confirm your booking. You might interest in to book private car transports from VM Travel. If you can’t process a booking online, there are several tourism desks at the airport, which you can book directly. The cost estimate 250,000 VND = 10 USD. With this option, passengers can feel extremely satisfied because our drivers speak English so you can communicate on the way. Especially, private car transfer from a travel agent will drop you off anywhere in Hue.

Hue Airport Vietnam

4.3: By Taxi

At Hue airport, there are many taxi drivers wait you to pick you up. You can easily find some brands such as Thanh Cong taxi, Mai Linh taxi, Go taxi, Vinasun taxi, etc. It will cost around 300,000 VND = 13 USD for one way trip. This option is also allow you have your own space to travel, drivers can not speak English so its challenging for communications. Even though travel with taxi is faster but it’s more expensive compare to other transports.
author: VM Travel

title: Travel Tips during Vietnamese Tet Holiday – Vietnam Lunar New Year
description: Vietnam Tet Holiday Tet Nguyen Dan which means “Feast of the First Morning of the First Day” falls on the first night of the new moon in the first...
content:
Vietnam Tet Holiday

Tet Nguyen Dan which means “Feast of the First Morning of the First Day” falls on the first night of the new moon in the first month of the Lunar Calendar. Thus, Tet Nguyen Dan or simply Tet is the Vietnamese Lunar New Year and the most important festival or holiday in Vietnam. This deeply rooted Vietnamese festival is usually celebrated for a week or even longer.

Travel Tips During Vietnamese Tet Holiday
1. What to Expect?
2. Transportation in these days
3. Accommodation Duration Vietnam Tet Holiday
4: Where to Eat?
5: Things to do to enjoy your trip on Vietnamese Tet Holiday?

Since Tet marks the arrival of spring in Vietnam, the date of celebration normally falls in January or February in the Gregorian calendar. As the most important Vietnamese holiday or festival. The public is typically given a week-long day off to be with their family and loved ones during the celebration. It has been the norm that Vietnamese who are living and working in the cities go home to their families in the provinces to celebrate this special occasion.

In Vietnamese Culture, Tet Holiday is a chance to start anew and leave all the worries and problems behind. Everyone will be cleaning their homes, cooking delicious Vietnamese cakes, buying new clothes and colorful flowers in preparation for the Tet festival. In Hanoi, motorbikes carrying kumquat trees would be a common sight while in Hue. People mostly buy chrysanthemums and in Ho Chi Minh, bright and colorful flowers are everywhere.
Vietnamese Tet Holiday is the grandest holiday for Vietnamese and for travelers. It is pivotal to consider this holiday too when planning as it may affect the whole trip to Vietnam experience.

What to Expect?

Tet Holiday is the most important celebration for all Vietnamese, thus expect that people would be leaving the cities and return to their hometowns to be with their family and friends. This would mean that bus terminals. Train stations and even airports would be crowded and getting a ride would definitely be a challenge.
Since locals are mostly leaving the cities before Tet holiday starts, expect that most stores will be closing down during these dates even in the big cities like Hanoi, Danang and Ho Chi Minh. Cities will be mostly quiet and with less people around. Several convenience stores will still be open on some days of the Tet Holiday though.
Tours would probably halt for a couple of days. Museums and most of the historical sites are closed during the holiday as well. Many temples and shrines are always open even during the holidays. Visiting them during Vietnamese Lunar New Year to ask for good health and luck would be a good thing to do.

Expect to find young Vietnamese on the streets on the second day of Tet Holiday, enjoying coffee or milk tea, playing cards and hanging out with their friends. Traditionally, the second day of Tet Holiday is reserved for friends. Visiting them at their homes or meeting them outside for a drink is common. For some locals, they are nice enough to invite travelers to join the celebration with them.
For Vietnamese who would be staying in the cities, they will fill the streets and would make as much noise as possible to ward off evil spirits. Expect to be hearing loud firecrackers, drums, bells, gongs and even the roaring sounds of motorbikes.

Transportation in these days

Since local transportation like buses, trains and planes would be sold out. It is inconvenient but buses would likely be overcrowded. People would be sitting on the floor uncomfortably during the whole trip just to get back to their hometown. If you are planning to get on a bus. You may book before the Tet Holiday and arrive at the bus station early to reserve your seat. Normally, the reservation does not necessarily mean that the bus company would reserve an actual seat for you.
This is the same scenario when taking the train as well. It would be crowded and a little inconvenient most especially when you’re traveling to a different city or province. You may book tickets beforehand, it is highly recommended to get a bed instead of seats when it’s a long trip. It is a little more convenient to stay in bed rather than sitting down with the crowd.

If within your budget, you can rent a private transfer to get to your next destination. Some travelers would talk to other travelers traveling to the same place and get a van or sedan for comfort and safety. It would be slightly more costly but definitely better than taking your chances of getting a seat in a crowded bus or train.
Extra planes would be provided during Tet Holidays but since a lot of people would be traveling as well. It is better to book weeks or even months before the date of travel. It is very crucial to not take your chances during this time of the year especially if your travel plan is not flexible.
Most travelers would simply rent motorbikes if they are planning to simply get to the next city. This would be one of the best ways to travel during Tet Holiday. Just make sure to get the motorbike checked before the holiday as it would be like asking for a miracle to find a repair shop in case something goes wrong with the motorbike.

Accommodation Duration Vietnam Tet Holiday

It is most likely that guesthouses, family owned hostels and hotels would be closing down during the Tet Holiday as well. For hotels and hostels that will be opened during the holidays, expect a higher price for accommodation. The price hike might go up to twice as much as its original price. This is quite understandable as these businesses would be paying the workers thrice their daily salary on these days.

For convenience, it is not enough to simply book your accommodation weeks or months prior to your trip. It is wise to get in touch with the hotel or hostel management a week before your supposed stay to make sure they will be open on these days. There are cases wherein hostel and hotel management would simply send their guests to another place because they did not sell out or suddenly no staff is willing to work on Tet Holiday.

Where to Eat?

It is a common misconception that all restaurants would be closing during the Tet Holiday. Travelers would most likely be buying their meals at a convenience store close to their hotel or hostel. But this is not always the case in the big cities like Hanoi, Danang, Hue, Hoi An, Nha Trang and Ho Chi Minh. Yes, it is undeniable that a lot of small and family owned shops and restaurants would be closing down but there are still places that would be open.

Normally in the backpackers area, shops and restaurants would still be open. Expect to see a different menu and pricing during the whole Tet Holiday week. Several international fast food chains are still open in Hanoi, Danang and Ho Chi Minh during these days, pricing might go higher but not as much as the other shops and restaurants. Checkout local malls as well as they are usually open even during the holidays too.

Things to do to enjoy your trip on Vietnamese Tet Holiday?
Prepare for your trip carefully. Research and make itineraries beforehand
Never take chances. Book accommodation, transportation and even get restaurant reservation weeks before the actual trip.
Make sure to get to the city a day or two before the actual Tet Holiday to avoid getting stuck in a far off place.
The flower market is a great place to be before the Tet Holiday. There would be hundreds of fresh and colorful flowers and Vietnamese people interacting. Flashing their smiles would be a beautiful sight to witness.
Though museums and historical sites would be closing down. There are other places to visit and does not necessarily need a tour guide. Research and list down these places you can visit on your own during the Tet Holiday.
If invited by locals, it is better to celebrate Tet Holiday with them than to stay inside your hotel room. If you are uncomfortable, politely decline the offer.
If you have plan to travel Hoi An, you will have a chance to see Lantern Festival in Hoi An
author: VM Travel

title: Best Restaurants in Hue City 2025
description: Restaurant In Hue – Top 7 Best Restaurant 2025 The imperial city of Hue is undoubtedly one of the most popular tourist attractions in Vietnam. The royal tombs, historical...
content:
Restaurant In Hue – Top 7 Best Restaurant 2025

The imperial city of Hue is undoubtedly one of the most popular tourist attractions in Vietnam. The royal tombs, historical buildings, and the beautifully designed Hue Citadel are all proudly showcased throughout the city. Due to its significance as the capital of Vietnam’s last empire, the Nguyen Dynasty, Hue Citadel is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Hue is popular not only because it was Vietnam’s ancient capital, but also because of its food. The majority of Hue cuisine is savory and rich in regional spices and herbs. There are two main types of Hue cuisine: royal dishes and rustic foods. Hue was the previous capital of the Nguyen Dynasty for a century, thus it seems sense that the city takes pleasure in its expertise of royal dishes. There is no other city that offers the same “royal cuisine” as the imperial metropolis, so it is quite difficult to match it. However, because they are delicious and meticulously prepared, just like royal foods, rustic cuisine is also difficult to overlook.

 Restaurant in Hue must to try
1: Madam Thu Restaurant
2: Cozy Restaurant
3: Nook Eatery
4: Hanh Restaurant
5: Bistro La Carambole
6: Les Jardins de La Carambole
7: Gecko Pub – Restaurant

The best restaurants in Hue, Viet Nam are where both tourists and locals go. These shortlisted restaurants are best when it comes to food, services and location.

1. Madam Thu Restaurant

Madam Thu Restaurant – Taste of Hue has been gaining a lot of attention since 2017. This restaurant deserves the top spot as it does not only serve great tasting local food but their service has only been superb.
High-ranking officials and local celebrities, including Daniel J. Kritenbrink, Assistant Secretary of State for East Asian and Pacific Affairs, have already dined at Madam Thu Restaurant. However, the restaurant consistently abides by its commitment to highlight Hue’s culture and cuisine. Celebrity or not, customers will get the same wonderful service. Madam Thu Restaurant believes that doing this will improve the customer’s overall experience and create a lasting great impression about the ancient city of Hue and its people.

Address: 45 Võ Thị Sáu, Phú Hội, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế 530000, Vietnam
Business Hours: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily

Book Table here: www.madamthu.com

2. Cozy Restaurant

Found in the middle of the city center, Cozy Restaurant is famous to foreign tourists because of the variety of dishes being served. From the local dishes to hamburgers and tortillas, Cozy Restaurant got it all. This is one of the best restaurants in Hue for tourists who are still getting used to the local cuisine.

Address: 27 Hà Nội, Phú Nhuận, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế, Vietnam
Business Hours: 11:50 AM – 10:00 AM Daily

3. Nook Eatery

Nook Eatery appeals to individuals who are more health conscious as the restaurant prepares dishes for vegan and non vegan. Though Nook Eatery has an entire Vietnamese menu, the restaurant is more well-known for dishes from other countries. Many would prefer having breakfast and an afternoon snack here. This is the best restaurant for comfort food in Hue.

Address: 3 Lương Thế Vinh, Phú Hội, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế 530000, Vietnam
Business Hours: 7:00 AM – 9:30 PM Daily

4. Hanh Restaurant

Hanh Restaurant is one of the oldest and best restaurants in Hue. The restaurant became known for its local dishes and just like Madam Thu Restaurant, Hanh Restaurant gets packed especially in the evening. Just a few meters away from the backpacker area of Hue, Hanh Restaurant is a must try when in Hue. A pro tip, better get here before 6 PM to secure a table.

Address: 11-15 Đường Phó Đức Chính, Phú Hội, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế, Vietnam
Business Hours: 10:00 am – 9:00 pm Daily

5. Bistro La Carambole

The best restaurant for European cuisine when visiting Hue is Bistro La Carambole, which is ideal for tourists looking to take a break from all the Vietnamese food. Bistro La Carambole seems to transfer its patrons to Europe while they are in the ancient city of Hue, giving it a distinctive vibe from all the other restaurants in the neighborhood.

Address: 18 Võ Thị Sáu, Phú Hội, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế, Vietnam
Business Hours: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM Daily

6. Les Jardins de La Carambole

The place is the best restaurant for French cuisine in Hue. Les Jardins de La Carambole, housed in a former French colonial house near to the historic Hue Citadel, exudes a refined and sophisticated ambiance. This Instagrammable restaurant is generally known for its French fare, but it also serves Vietnamese food.

Address: 32 Đặng Trần Côn, Thuận Hoà, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế, Vietnam
Business Hours: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM Daily

7. Gecko Pub – Restaurant

Gecko Restaurant, which is situated in the center of Hue City Walking Street, is the ideal spot to unwind in the late afternoon. The restaurant serves both Vietnamese and Mexican cuisines. Younger travelers who want to dine and drink affordably while taking in the nighttime bustle of the street are the primary customers for Gecko Pub.

Address: 9 Phạm Ngũ Lão, Phú Hội, Thành phố Huế, Thừa Thiên Huế, Vietnam
Business Hours: 3:00 PM – 11:00 PM Daily

You can join tour to taste all of Hue Cuisine
1. HUE STREET FOOD TOUR BY CYCLO
2. HUE CITY TOUR FULL DAY
3. TASTE OF HUE FOOD BY WALKING TOUR
4. HUE COOKING CLASS TOUR

title: Visiting Hoi An
description: Visiting Hoi An with 9 Famous Tourist Attraction The ancient town of Hoi An is a remarkably well-preserved depiction of a South-East Asian trading port from the 15th to...
content:
Visiting Hoi An with 9 Famous Tourist Attraction

The ancient town of Hoi An is a remarkably well-preserved depiction of a South-East Asian trading port from the 15th to the 19th century. This UNESCO declared World Heritage Site is located in the Quang Nam Province of Central Vietnam.
In addition to the cultural complex itself. The ancient town features more attractions that tourists can consider when visiting Hoi An. These nearby historical locations, which have influenced Hoi An both culturally and economically, are well worth seeing

Best Places To Visiting Hoi An
1: Hoi An Coconut Forest
2: My Son Holy Land
3: Tra Que Vegetable Village Hoi An
4: Cu Lao Cham Island
5: Hoai River Hoi An
6: An Bang Beach
7: Thanh Ha Pottery Village
8: Hoi An Memories Land
9: Hoi An Old Town
9.1: Hoi An Japanese Bridge
9.2: Phuc Kien Assembly Hall
9.3: Central Market Hoi an
9.4: Tan Ky Old House
9.5: Ong Pagoda
1. Hoi An Coconut Forest

The Bay Mau Coconut Forest is located in Cam Thanh Commune, Quang Nam Province, about 5 kilometres from the well-known trading port of Hoi An. During the resistance movement against the French and later the Americans. The Cam Thanh Coconut Forest was once known as the base camp of the Vietnam Revolutionary Army. The coconut forest in Hoi An served as a haven as well as a strategic battlefield for the smaller in number Vietnam Revolutionary Army.
As the villagers offer not only the coconut boat rides exploring the Cam Thanh coconut forest but which also thanks to a number of gimmicks. The Hoi An Coconut Forest has been experiencing a surge in visitors. One of these is the coconut boat performance, in which a paddler quickly turns the boat around while providing tourists with a spectacular and thrilling sight. Visitors can also get complimentary coconut leaf origami. One experience to think about when visiting Hoi An is catching a glimpse of the simple life of the fishing village.

Recommended Tour: Cam Thanh Coconut Village Tour

Explore Cam Thanh Coconut village

2. My Son Holy Land

The historic Cham Kingdom built the Hindu temple complex known as My Son Sanctuary, about 40 kilometers away from Hoi An. It was constructed between the fourth and the fourteenth centuries AD, during the rule of Champa kings. The site was used for the royals’ religious rituals. My Son Sanctuary was grand and of the utmost importance to the history of Hinduism in Southeast Asia since it served as the political and religious capital of the Champa Kingdom.
My Son Sanctuary was destroyed after the Champa Kingdom fell and was later rediscovered by a French explorer in 1898. The site had been restored since 1937, but the Vietnam War’s heavy bombardment in the region caused significant destruction. However, My Son Sanctuary’s ruins are still acknowledged as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Recommended Tour: My Son Sanctuary Tour – Private Tour

Discovery My Son Sanctuary in Hoi An

3. Tra Que Vegetable Village Hoi An

Approximately 4 kilometres from Hoi An lies the little island where Tra Que Vegetable Village is located. Following the introduction of Hoi An cooking classes for, the vegetable village of Tra Que began drawing tourists. A trip to a neighbouring vegetable village, like the well-known Tra Que Vegetable village. Is typically included in cooking classes so that participants can plant and pick herbs and vegetables that will be used in the Hoi An cooking class.

Recommend Tour: Hoi An Cycling and Cooking Class Tour

4. Cu Lao Cham Island

Cham Islands, sometimes referred to as Cu Lao Cham, is about 20 kilometers away from Hoi An Old Town. Visitors should be able to get to the islands by a 20-minute boat ride. The Cham Islands are included in the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve Cu Lao Cham – Hoi An, which safeguards the diverse marine life.
As the Cham islands provide superb snorkeling and diving activities. It has become a popular destination for all day excursions when visiting Hoi An. On its largest island, Hon Lao, there are also Buddhist temples and a museum dedicated to the Cham people. Who are thought to have been the initial settlers of Hoi An.

Discovery Cham Island from Da Nang & Hoi An

5. Hoai River Hoi An

The Hoai River runs the length of the historical site of Hoi An. Boat cruises along the Hoai River are available for those who would like to experience Hoi An in an unique manner. Along the river, dozens of boats pass, and spectators may see locals going about their everyday routines and carrying on business. The river glows at night with a variety of lanterns strung across the nearby streets and atop the boats. Visitors will find Hoai River Hoi An at its best during Full Moon Festivals.

6. An Bang Beach

An Bang Beach located in Cam An ward is approximately 5 kilometers away from the center of Hoi An. Due to its stunning cool blue waves, serene atmosphere, and lack of nearby businesses. The quiet white sand An Bang beach has recently emerged as the crowd’s favorite and worth visiting when in Hoi An.

7. Thanh Ha Pottery Village

Hoi An Ancient Town is only 3.4 kilometers away from Thanh Ha Village. It is a village close to the picturesque and well-known Thu Bon River thus. Visitors can get there by boat, motorcycle, vehicle, or even bicycle. The fact that Thanh Ha Pottery Village provides a real sense of Vietnamese culture and traditions is probably one of its best features.
Thanh Ha Pottery Village is a significant spot to see while visiting to Hoi An because of its long and rich history. When a Nguyen Emperor asked skilled and knowledgeable potters from Thanh Ha to assist in the construction of the Imperial City and the Hue Citadel. Which is still standing today, the town gained national attention. Since the artisans demonstrated their skill at these events, a growing number of people became aware of the Thanh Ha Pottery village and eventually spread to different cities as well as surrounding Southeast Asian nations and certain European nations, including France and the Netherlands.

8. Hoi An Memories Land

The Hoi An Impression Theme Park is unlike any other parks in Vietnam. The distinctive theme park is located at 200 Nguyen Tri Phuong, Cam Nam, Hoi An, Quang Nam. In contrast to other theme parks, Hoi An Impression Theme Park’s evening performances are its main attraction. The traditional ao dai of Vietnam is performed in the real world using cutting-edge lighting, staging, and audio effects. Through these, Hoi An’s 400-year-old history is depicted in all its radiance and prosperity. Making it a wonderful diversion from the typical evening spent merely wandering through Hoi An Old Town’s shophouses.

9. Hoi An Old Town

The Hoi An Ancient Town is a particularly well-preserved example of a 15th to the 19th century South-East Asian trading port. This distinctive cultural complex’s architecture and street plan demonstrate the fusion of both local and international influences.
A mix of cultures and styles, from wooden Chinese shophouses, assembly halls and temples to vibrant French colonial structures, beautiful Vietnamese tube houses. The famed Japanese Covered Bridge with its pagoda, reflects the Hoi An’s multicultural history.

9.1 Hoi An Japanese Bridge

The Japanese Bridge in Hoi An is a skillfully constructed structure that serves as a prominent symbol of the historical site. It is claimed that the Japanese community supported the construction of the Hoi An Japanese Bridge and its pagoda, known as the Cau Pagoda, in the 1590s to connect it with the Chinese neighborhoods.

9.2 Phuc Kien Assembly Hall

Phuc Kien Assembly Hall is located at 46 Tran Phu Street and one of the most popular assembly halls within Hoi An old town. The Phuc Kien Assembly Hall was constructed around 1690 and originally served as a social gathering place for the Chinese ethnic group from Fujian, China. However, it was eventually converted into a temple for the Goddess of the Sea.

9.3 Central Market Hoi An

The Hoi An Central Market is just next to the iconic Hoi An Ancient Town. Visiting Hoi an Central Market gives a different experience to both residents and tourists. Locals and international visitors typically purchase their goods, food supplies, other essentials, and souvenirs here. This the place where tourists are most likely to find genuine culinary delights. As White Rose, Cau Lau noodles, and Mi Quang noodles.

9.4 Tan Ky Old House

The old house of Tan Ky is located at 101 Nguyen Thai Hoc Street within the heritage site of Hoi An. Tan Ky Old House is a fascinating merchant’s house from the 18th century that encompasses more than 200 years of history and can be deemed a “living museum”.

9.5 Ong Pagoda

The Ong Pagoda, also known as the Quan Cong Temple, is situated on Tran Phu Street in the historical site of Hoi An. Quan Cong, an ancient Chinese general who lived during the “Three Warring Kingdoms,” was honored at the Ong Pagoda. The Hoi an Ong Pagoda was constructed in the seventeenth century and has undergone numerous restorations.

Other tours you might be interested in
1. EXPLORE MY SON SANCTUARY FROM HOI AN – PRIVATE TOUR
2. CHAM ISLAND TOUR
3. EXPLORE CAM THANH COCONUT VILLAGE FROM HOI AN – PRIVATE TOUR
4. HOI AN CITY TOUR 1 DAY – PRIVATE TOUR

title: HOW TO GET TO HOI AN FROM DA NANG
description: How To Get From Da Nang To Hoi An Da Nang and Hoi An are the 2 famous cities in Central Vietnam and attract a lot of tourists coming...
content:
How To Get From Da Nang To Hoi An

Da Nang and Hoi An are the 2 famous cities in Central Vietnam and attract a lot of tourists coming to visit every day. These cities are close to each other, which makes the need of transportation between them are very high. Da Nang has a busy international airport, so most of tourists will stay in Da Nang first, then will find a way to get to Hoi An from Da Nang on the next days. So how to get to Hoi An from Da Nang? Let’s find the answer with us!

1: Where is Da Nang
2: Where is Hoi An
3: How far from Da Nang to Hoi An
4: Best way to get to Hoi An from Da Nang
4.1: By Private Car
4.2: By Bus
4.3: By Taxi
4.4: By Grap
4.5: By Limousine Bus
4.6: By Motorbike
5: Which is Tourist Attraction from Da Nang to Hoi An
1. Where is Da Nang

Da Nang is a city in Central Vietnam. Besides Ha Noi and Ho Chi Minh city, Da Nang is the third one in Vietnam that is well-known and attractive for tourists all over the world. It is Vietnam’s fifth largest city and the largest city in Central Vietnam.
Da Nang city is about 100km to the south of Hue city and about 30km to the North of the charming Hoi an ancient town. This makes Da Nang become an ideal place to start the journey to visit Central Vietnam

2. Where is Hoi An

Hoi An is an old town in Quang Nam province. This small town is famous for it’s spectacularly well-preserved old trading port. And it is one of the must destination in Southeast Asia due to its unique beauty, delicious cuisine and rich culture. A trip to Vietnam in general and to Central Vietnam in particular without a visiting to Hoi An will be a miss.

3. How far from Da Nang to Hoi An

The distance from DaNang Airport to HoiAn is 30 km. Depending on the way you choose to transfer, the trip from Da Nang to Hoi An can take from 45 to 90 minutes

4. Best way to get to Hoi An from Da Nang

Because Hoi an is very close to Da Nang, there are many ways to get to Hoi An from Da Nang. Some transport options to choose such as by private car, by bus, grab, taxi, & motorbike, … Depend on your budget, time and hobby, you can choose the suitable one to make your trip more meaningful.

4.1 By Private Car

You can easily take a private car to Hoi An from local travel companies. This is the best way to get to Hoi an from Da nang as the price will be always fixed and very safe. You are also supported at any time by the company to ensure that you get the most suitable car and driver.
Another reason why this is the best option is that there are many types of cars from 4 – 45 seats to choose. This will meet all the customer’s needs and sizes.
This is also the most convenient and the fastest option as you can stop anywhere you like on the way, maybe for taking photos, for a drink or for lunch.

Recomended Best Transport: Da Nang to Hoi An By Private Car 

4.2 By Bus

This is a good option for tourists who don’t have much budget. The bus number 1 from Da Nang to Hoi An is a local bus also known as the Yellow Bus. This bus departs every 20 minutes, starting from 5:30AM to 6:00 PM every day. You can easily take it at Da Nang Central Bus station. Price for a bus ticket is about 25.000 vnd/ pax. You don’t need to buy the ticket in advance, just pay when you are on the bus.
However, there will be so many people in the bus, it will be very noisy, hot & uncomfortable. If you don’t like crowd, you shouldn’t choose this option.

4.3 By Taxi

You can take a taxi to get to Hoi An at anywhere in Da nang city. You can choose a 4 seater taxi or a 7 seater taxi depending on the size of your group. The price will be calculated by kms. And it will be from 450.000 vnd for 1 way transfer. You can think about Vina taxi (+84 511 368 6868), Mai Linh taxi (+84 511 352 5252),…. If your group has more than 5 people, choose another better option.

4.4 By Grap

There are 2 types of Grab so that you can choose: Grab Car and Grab Bike. All you have to do is to download the app Grab on your phone and book the car option or motorbike option. You can see where your driver is through the app and the cost of the trip showed on the screen. Normally, the cost is from 5usd for a Grab Bike and from 15usd for a Grab car depending on your location.

4.5 By Limousine Bus

If a local bus is not suitable for you, then you can think about a Limousine Bus with better quality and less people. Price is from 6usd/ ticket for a direct trip. Some Limousine Bus companies you can check are Dong Hanh Bus Company, Cuc Tung Bus Company, HAV Travel, Empire Limousine,…. You can book the tickets directly on their website or book via travel companies.

4.5 By Motorbike

An adventurous way to transfer between these two cities is riding motorbike. You can rent a motorbike easily from your hotel, then ride yourselves to Hoi an and stop at anywhere on the way you want to take photos,.. Price for 1 motorbike for 1 day is also cheap, just about 5-7 usd/motorbike, and you will have to pay for petrol,.. If you cannot ride, it is a big problem with the crowded streets in Vietnam.

5. Which is Tourist Attraction:

If you think that the trip from Da Nang to Hoi An is just a boring trip, you are wrong. If you use private car, taxi or motorbike options, you can totally add some other places to the trip such as Linh Ung pagoda, Marble Mountains and My Khe beach. The private car can stop for you to visit those places without limiting the visit time. Normal price for the whole trip is from 38usd per a sedan. For taxi, it will be more expensive.
Hope that with this article, you will find out the best way to travel from Da nang to Hoi An. Enjoy your trip in Vietnam!
author: VM Travel

title: Hoi An In Viet Nam
description: Hoi An Ancient Town In Viet Nam – UNESCO World Heritage Centre Hoi An in Viet Nam is a Old Town located in Central of Viet Nam. It was...
content:
Hoi An Ancient Town In Viet Nam – UNESCO World Heritage Centre

Hoi An in Viet Nam is a Old Town located in Central of Viet Nam. It was listed as a World Cultural Heritage by Unesco in 1999. Where is Famous Place for Tourist when they have plan to travel Viet Nam.

1: Introduction of Hoi An in Viet Nam
2: Where is Hoi An
3: Weather in Hoi An
4: Best Time to Visit Hoi An in Viet Nam
5: Hoi An Airport (No Airport – Must get from Da Nang Airport)
6: How to get to Hoi An in Viet Nam
6.1: How to get from Da Nang to Hoi An
6.2: How to get from Hue to Hoi An
6.3: How to get from Ha Noi to Hoi An
6.4: How to get from Ho Chi Minh to Hoi An
1. Introduction of Hoi An in Viet Nam

The Hoi An Ancient Town, which dates from the 15th to the 19th century, is a remarkably well-preserved illustration of a South-East Asian trading port. The architecture and the street layout of this distinct cultural complex exhibit the blend of local and international influences.
Hoi An Ancient Town has maintained its traditional timber architecture and townscape. The historical landscape backdrop, which includes a coastal environment with a river, shoreline, dunes, and islands, is similarly unaltered. This is why in 1985, historic Hoi An Ancient Town was designated as a National Cultural Heritage Site, and in 1999, UNESCO declared it a World Heritage Site.
The Hoi An Old Town is a testament of the blending of native and foreign cultures (mostly Chinese and Japanese with later influences from Europe), which together formed this exceptional survival. According to history, Hội An (會安) which means “peaceful meeting place” from Sino-Vietnamese was known historically as Faifo. This word is derived from Vietnamese Hội An phố which translates to “the town of Hoi An” and was shortened to “Hoi-pho”, and then finally to “Faifo”.
Hoi An is a name which truly gives justice to this historic commercial port. It has served as a “peaceful meeting place” for merchants from various countries centuries ago and for global spectators now. The Ancient Town of Hoi An is yet another proof that Vietnam truly has a Timeless Charm.

Introduction Hoi An in Viet Nam

2. Where is Hoi An

The Hoi An old town is situated in Quang Nam Province in Central Vietnam. One of the three renowned cities in Central Vietnam is the ancient town of Hoi An. Both the well-known urban beach city of Danang and the historical Imperial City of Hue are nearby.
Close to the mouth of the Thu Bon River on the north bank is where you will find Hoi An Ancient Town. This small town of Hoi An has grown to become one of the most well-liked tourist destinations, drawing visitors from all continents and with a variety of travel preferences

Hoi An Ancient Town
3. Weather in Hoi An

Hoi-An experiences tropical weather because it lies in the centre of Vietnam. Despite this, the temperature somehow doesn’t vary significantly from season to season. The first five months of the year (January to May) are typically milder and less likely to rain.
Summer lasts from June to August, with temperatures averaging 35°C (94°F) at their highest point around July. Storms are likely to occur frequently in Central Vietnam from September through December. Hoi An has occasionally experienced flooding in the past during these months’ strong rainfall. The wettest month of the year is October, therefore travellers must carefully check the weather forecasts before making trip plans.

Autumn in Hoi An
4. Best Time to Visit Hoi An in Viet Nam

Visiting the Ancient Town of Hoi An is typically a wise choice for certain tourists. However, taking a number of things into account can make the overall trip experience even further.
Weather can be erratic in Hoi An. Sudden heavy rain can produce flooding in the ancient trading port or scorching heat make it walk around the charming little old town of Hoi An. Because of the weather, it is more ideal to travel to Hoi An between February and May. Since the slightly milder weather makes it easier to go from shop to shop and temple to temple inside the South-East Asian trading port town of Hoi An, April is likely to see more visitors than any other month.
Pro tip: The Hoi An Lantern Full Moon Festival, which takes place each full moon cycle, is another attraction. The festivities pay tribute to the ancestors. Flowers, lanterns, candles, and fruits are traded for prosperity and good luck. Make sure to consider the dates of the Hoi An Lantern Full Moon Festival while arranging travel plans to Hoi An

Lantern festival in Hoi An
5. Hoi An Airport (No Airport – Must get from Da Nang Airport)

Hoi An being a small old town with great intentions in preserving its museum-like South-East Asian trading port, has no airport. There just isn’t place for an airport in the historic town of Hoi An due to its size. It is believed that having no airport within the area would minimize damages to the well-preserved Hoi An World Heritage Site.
As a well-known trading port from 15th through the 19th century, there has not been much of changes in terms of transportation options in Hoi An, Vietnam. Travellers wanting to get to Hoi An via plane can go to Danang Airport which caters both domestic and international flights. Hoi An is only approximately 30 kilometres from Danang, therefore getting there from the airport in Danang City would be simple because there are numerous ways to do so.

6. How to get to Hoi An in Vietnam

Map from Hue or Da Nang to Hoi An
6.1 How to get from Da Nang to Hoi An

Da Nang is the nearest city to Hoi An with approximate distance of 30 kilometers. The coastal city of Danang has its own airport, dock ports for cruise ships and provincial trains which Hoi An lack of. Tourists traveling via plane, cruise ships or trains will have to go to Da Nang first. Danang offers wide variety of transportation means to get to any cities in Vietnam or even other countries.

The best way to Hoi An

Bus or shuttle would be the cheapest and most feasible means of transportation especially for solo travellers from Danang to Hoi An. The price of a bus ticket for a 1.5-hour trip is only 30,000 VND. While a one-way trip from Da Nang to Hoi An may cost between 120,000 and 150,000 VND, depending on the shuttle bus company.
Motorbike from Danang to Hoi An is also possible only if traveling light. This may appeal to younger and young at heart travelers as taking a motorbike is an exciting and very affordable way to get to Hoi An from Danang. Motorbike rental can cost from 180,000 VND – 300,000 VND depending on the bike and if pick up would be needed.
If traveling with a lot of stuff, it should be simple and more comfortable to hail a cab from Da Nang to Hoi An. However, there have been recent reports of scams, thus it is strongly advised to use reputable taxi service companies when going from Danang to Hoi An. It should only cost 320,000 VND to 400,000 VND depending on the hotel location in Hoi An.
Private car transfers have been more appealing to individuals, couples or groups wanting to go to Hoi An from Da nang. For health safety reasons, more tourists are booking private car services considering that the cost may not be that far off from getting a taxi. A 4-seater private car to Hoi an from Da nang may cost from 350,000 VND to 650,000 VND depending on route plans.

Transport	Estimated Time	Price
By Bus	1.5 hours	120.000 VND
By Motorbike	2 hours	200.000 – 300.000 VND
By Taxi	1.5 hours	320.000 – 400.000 VND
By Private Car	1.5 hours	350.000 – 650.000 VND
6.2 How to get from Hue to Hoi An

The charming ancient Imperial City of Hue to Hoi An is only 126 kilometers away. Being one of the biggest cities in Central Vietnam, Hue has airport and train station aside from the usual modes of transport in the country.
The iconic Marble Mountains in Danang, the famed Hai Van Pass, the tranquil Lang Co Beach, and Lap An Lagoon – famous for its fresh oyster farms and picturesque Bach Ma Mountain, – can all be seen along the route from Hue to Hoi An.

The best way to Hoi An

As usual, bus would still be the cheapest and most feasible means of transportation when traveling alone from Hue to Hoi An. The price of a one-way bus ticket for a 3.5-hour trip from Da Nang to Hoi An may cost between 120,000 and 150,000 VND, depending on the bus company.
Motorbikes are a thrilling way to get from Hue to Hoi An if you’re travelling light and are willing to try new things. Depending on the motorbike and whether luggage transfers are required, the cost of renting a motorbike ranges from 500,000 to 800,000 VND.
Hailing a taxi may not be practical at this point however, private car transfer to Hoi An from Hue is the most popular. Aside from health and safety considerations, private car services have been among the best ways to travel from Hue to Hoi An because free stops at well-known tourist attractions are provided. Somehow, it transforms into a private tour. Depending on the route used and the type of vehicle, a private car from Hue to Hoi An may cost between 1,500,000 and 1,750,000 VND.

Train travel from Hue to Hoi An is another interesting method. The countryside and the enthralling beauty of Hue Province’s terrain and coastline areas are best viewed from the railway route. Unfortunately, the train will have to halt at Danang, forcing passengers to take a taxi, board a bus, or rent a motorcycle to get to Hoi An. Depending on the type of seat, a train ticket from Hue to Danang might cost anywhere between 62,000 VND and 105,000 VND.

Detailed price list of vehicles
Transport	Estimated Time	Price
By Bus	3.5 hours	120.000 – 150.000 VND

title: Is Ninh Binh Worth Visiting?
description: Ninh Binh – A Diverse Tourist Destination 1: How far from Ha Noi to Ninh Binh? 2: How many days do you need in Ninh Binh? 3: When should...
content:
Ninh Binh – A Diverse Tourist Destination
1: How far from Ha Noi to Ninh Binh?
2: How many days do you need in Ninh Binh?
3: When should i go to Ninh Binh?
4: Which sightseeing tourist should visit in Ninh Binh?
4.1: Trang An Complex
4.2: Mua Caves
4.3: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital
4.4: Bai Dinh Pagoda
4.5: Bich Dong Pagoda
4.6: Cuc Phuong National Park

Is Ninh Binh Worth Visiting? Tourists often ask this question when planning to travel in northern Vietnam. And the answer is Yes, absolutely yes. It is a must destination to travel in your vacation. There are majestic caves and mossy temples to explore. Ninh Binh shows the endless beauty with rice fields, coming here you will visit many destinations such as Trang An Complex, Hoa Lu Capital, Mua caves, Cuc Phuong National Park,…etc.
Ninh Binh increasingly becomes a popular destination in Northern Vietnam. This place is known as “ Ha Long Bay on land” and it lies in the Red River Delta’s southernmost regions. In comparison with Sapa and Ha Giang, Ninh Binh shows the beauty of endåless rice fields, towering limestone mountains and spectacular caves. There are many cultural and historical relics to explore especially Trang An complex which is a UNESCO world heritage. For first-time visitors, you are probably wondering: “ Is Ninh Binh worth visiting?”. The answer is “yes” and absolutely a firm “yes”.

1. How far from Ha Noi to Ninh Binh?

Ninh Binh is a province in Red river delta. It shares border with Hoa Binh and Nam Dinh in the north, Ha Nam in Northeast, Thanh Hoa in the south and west.
Being away from Hanoi 94 km, tourists can travel easily by bus, private car or by train to Ninh Binh. Approximately, it takes around 2 to 3 hours to reach the destination from Hanoi.

If you want to get Private Car with English Speaking Driver, You can BOOK VIA HERE

2. How many days do you need in Ninh Binh?

Ninh Binh has many activities to do. Spending 1 day will be very rush and short to discover all the sights in this destination. In my suggestion, 2 days is a perfect time to spend your holiday here.
Within 2 days discover Ninh Binh, you can visit many attractions such as Mua Caves, Bich Dong pagoda, Tam Coc Boat Tour, Trang An complex and Bai Dinh pagoda.

When should i go to Ninh Binh?

Ninh Binh’s weather is comprised of four seasons. It is hot, humid and contains high rainfall from May to October. Especially, in August, the precipitation reaches its highest level and the region is usually vulnerable to typhoons and strong storms in September to October. Meanwhile, from November to April, the weather is cooler and dry.
The best time to visit Ninh Binh is during May and July, which offer you the chance to admire the natural beauty of this place. Apart from the harvest season with the beautiful golden paddle fields, another advantage of visiting Ninh Binh in June is that you can see lotus in Tam Coc – Bich Dong.

Which sightseeing tourist should visit in Ninh Binh?

There are many attractive destinations in Ninh Binh which I can’t recommend enough. It is an oasis for nature lovers. Vietnam first capital established here, and today the area is a protected UNESCO World Heritage Site, full of mossy pagodas, striking limestone karsts, and surreal landscapes. Below are my suggestions for sightseeing.

Trang An Complex

Proudly, Trang An Complex is a mysterious natural and cultural area, not affected by human activities. This destination is listed on world UNESCO heritage. There are the pristine forests, the clear lakes, rivers and streams, the fresh air, the quiet villages and only sound and colors of nature. Visitors to Trang An will experience the feeling of going back in time to the golden beauty of Vietnam history, immersing themselves in traditional spiritual culture, or floating on small boats that are popular with locals, contemplating strange rocks and caves, enjoying a pure atmosphere, experiencing each level of emotions and exploring the distant roots of human life.

Mua Caves

Mua caves is a destination which you can’t miss when you visit this ancient capital. Here, you can explore panoramic view of nature and majestic limestone mountains. When coming to Mua Cave, you will be able to test your endurance when climbing to the top of the mountain through 486 stone steps, and from the top of the mountain we can admire the whole beauty of Tam Coc area with wings, vast and green rice fields.

Hoa Lu Ancient Capital

Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is the capital of an ancient Vietnamese Kingdom called Dai Co Viet in the 10th century. This small Kingdom covered an area of only 300 hectares, and reigned from the 10th century, during the Dinh and Le dynasties, to the 11th century, during the Ly Dynasty. The Kingdom was enclosed by a citadel. The citadel and the Yen Ngua limestone hills provided a good defence for this kingdom against the Chinese.

Bai Dinh Pagoda

Bai Dinh Pagoda is a spiritual landscape located in the complex of Bai Dinh – Trang An eco-tourism area with a history of more than 1000 years associated with the land of many feudal dynasties from Dinh, Tien, Le and Ly Dynasty.
Not only do the marvelous sceneries make you dote on it, it also elicits a precious land with ancient historical relics of ancient Vietnam which is the second thing you should know. Bai Dinh Padoda includes both of majestic sceneries and spirit values. This is the largest spiritual pagoda in Viet Nam and one of the must-see places in Ninh Binh.

Bich Dong Pagoda

It is one of the most magnificent grottos in Vietnam, the Bich Dong pagoda is a spot of natural beauty in the Northern provinces. This cave contributes to spiritual tourism development due to its pagodas inside the cave, like other Ninh Binh caves. A 3-kilometer distance to the east of Tam Coc, Bich Dong pagoda, literally the pagoda of Emerald Cave, is situated in the Truong Yen range. Truong Yen range lies in the valley of rice paddy fields and leans on Bich Dong mountain. The only route to get to the pagoda is to step into the steep stone but what is waiting for you is definitely worth visiting.

Cuc Phuong National Park

It is Vietnam’s largest national park and one of the most important biodiversity sites in the country. It is home to hundreds of species of flora and fauna. The park can be visited from Hanoi as a day trip or visitors can stay at the park’s lodgings for a longer stay. Visiting the park is a terrific opportunity to get a close look at Vietnam’s nature. Fees generated from tourism help protect the parks wildlife and improve the local economy.

title: Hue Tombs Tour – All Infomation of Famous Royal Tomb in Hue
description: Hue Tombs Tour – Royal Tombs In Hue City 1: Hue tombs tour – What is it? 2: How many Hue royal tombs in Hue city 3: Which royal...
content:
Hue Tombs Tour – Royal Tombs In Hue City
1: Hue tombs tour – What is it?
2: How many Hue royal tombs in Hue city
3: Which royal tomb the traveler should visit in Hue city
4: How much for entrance fee each hue royal tomb
5: How to visit Hue Tomb Tour 1 day
1. Hue tombs tour – What is it?

Hue tombs tour is a tour organized to take the tourists to many royal tombs in Hue city. Giving them a chance to explore the interesting history of Nguyen Dynasty and their architecture with the best itinerary, schedule and cost. There are many Hue royal tombs tours including full day tours, half day tours, group tours and private tours suitable for all the needs of the tourists.

2. How many Hue royal tombs in Hue city

Hue city is the imperial capital in central Vietnam that was flourishing during the Nguyen Dynasty. Hue city attracts a lot of tourists all over the world also thanks to the remaining monuments of that time, especially the system of royal tombs.
Though there were 13 Emperors during the Nguyen Dynasty, there are just 7 royal tombs were built due to many political –economy circumstances. The tombs are in many different styles, but share many common characteristics. Six royal tombs are located to the southeast of the Hue Citadel on the other side of the Perfume River and a single tomb is on the same side (Minh Mang tomb).
The tombs are now to bury 10 Kings of the Nguyen Dynasty. Below is the list of 7 royal tombs:

Hue Tomb Tour – Private Tour
Private Tour
1 Day
Hue City
Tomb Tour
Tour Guide
Private Tour
Free Ticket
Drink Water

From

15 USD

Detail
2.1 Gia Long Tomb (Thien Tho Lang) : The best tomb for intrepid adventurers

Gia Long tomb is the tomb of the first emperor of the Nguyen Dynasty: Gia Long King. The tomb is beside the Perfume River, between the lush pine forests on Dai Thien Tho Mountain, surrounded by mountains, rivers and trees. It is nature that creates the majestic look for the entire structure. However, the tomb is the farthest one of the 7 royal tombs which make it somewhat inaccessible and isn’t much visited.

Gia Long Tomb
2.2: Minh Mang Tomb ( Hieu Lang) : The best for history and culture buffs

This tomb is the eternal home of Emperor Minh Mang. It is located on mount Cam Khe, on the west bank of Perfume River, about 12km from Hue center.
The tomb is different from the rest of imperial tombs because of its artistic ideological values. It is a perfect combination of nature and structures where architecture fits harmoniously into the surrounding landscape. This make it unmatched among tombs in Hue. This harmony reveals a lot about personality of Minh Mang emperor and his characteristics during his reign.

Minh Mang Tomb
2.3: Thieu Tri Tomb ( Xuong Lang): The best for escaping the crowds

This tomb is the resting place of Thieu Tri Emperor – the 3rd Emperor of Nguyen Dynasty
It is located in Cu Chanh village, Thuy Bang commune, Huong Thuy district, about 8km south of Hue citadel. Thieu Tri Tomb is considered a combination of two architectural models of his two predecessors’ tombs: Gia Long Tomb; Minh Mang tomb.

Thieu Tri Tomb
2.4: Tu Duc Tomb ( Khiem Lang) ( the most poetic tomb): The best for dreamers and romantics

Tu Duc tomb is the tomb of Tu Duc King. It is located in an immense pine valley about 8km from Hue city. This tomb was designed by Emperor Tu Duc himself before his death. It is one of the most beautiful tombs of royal architecture of the Nguyen Dynasty with large park, beautiful lake and green pine trees. This is a poetic jewel set in an intricate tomb.

Tu Duc Tomb
2.5: Duc Duc Tomb ( An Lang) : The best for quick sightseeing

Duc Duc tomb is the resting place of 3 kings: Duc Duc King, Thanh Thai King & Duy Tan King
It is located in Tay Nhat village, An Cuu village, less than 2 km from the city. This is the simplest and modest tomb of the 7 royal tombs.

Duc Duc Tomb
2.6: Dong Khanh Tomb  ( Tu Lang) The best for countryside vistas

This tomb is the resting place of Emperor Dong Khanh. It is located between Thieu Tri Tomb and Tu Duc tomb. This tomb is the smallest of the seven royal tombs in Hue, with stained glass windows and terra-cotta reliefs mixing with typical Eastern design influences.

Dong Khanh Tomb
2.7: Khai Dinh Tomb ( Ung Lang) : The best for something completely different

Khai Dinh Tomb is the resting place of Emperor Khai Dinh, the second last king of the Nguyen Dynasty. This tomb is situated in Chau Chu Mountains, about 10km away from Hue city. It is a small tomb, but most costly and most sophisticated in architecture in comparison with other tombs of Nguyen royals. This tomb is a masterpiece of glass and terracotta in Hue.

Khai Dinh Tomb
3. Which royal tomb the traveler should visit in Hue city
A stay in Hue would not be complete without a visit to the stunning Hue royal Tombs. However, which royal tomb the traveler should visit in Hue?
All the royal tombs are nice and worth visiting, however, not all of them are well-known with tourists. Among 7 royal tombs of the Nguyen Emperors in Hue, there are 3 most popular tombs: Khai Dinh tomb, Minh Mang tomb and Tu Duc tomb. And these 3 tombs are added to most of the Hue city tours or Hue tombs tour.
If you have just 1 day in Hue and are interested in visiting the tombs, a royal tomb tour with these 3 famous tombs (Khai Dinh, Minh mang, Tu Duc tomb) and Citadel,… will be a good choice for you. This is because these tombs are still in good condition, have good location and size and have nice architecture that suitable for arranging a full day or half day tour. And of course, most of tourists coming to Hue will choose these tombs for convenience.
Travel Budget Recommendation for Tombs in Hue
The entrance tickets for the 3 popular tombs are so high compared to the rest. To save the cost, but still have a chance to visit the tombs, you can choose a Hue tomb tour with visiting the less famous tombs (Thieu Tri Tomb, Dong Khanh tomb; Gia Long tomb). You can take the tour by car, by motorbike, and even by bicycle.
Duc Duc tomb is close and easy to reach, but it is small and has an extremely simple and modest architecture. It is also not on the way to other tombs. Therefore, this tomb seems not to be attractive for tourists. If you want a not crowded place to visit, this tomb is an ideal place. The fresh air without any crowds here will also make you feel very comfortable and peaceful.
In generally, all the tombs are worth visiting because of their value of history and architecture. And you should choose the most suitable tombs depending on your budget and time. To be more convenient, you should choose the most 3 popular tombs ( Khai dinh tomb, Minh mang tomb and Tu Duc tomb) as they are included in most of Hue city tours. If you don’t have much budget or do not like the crowd, you should choose the less popular tombs.
4. How much for entrance fee each hue royal tomb

Most of the tombs charge entrance fee and prices of the tickets are updated yearly. The price of the tickets for each tomb is also different depending on their popularity. So how much for entrance fee each hue royal tomb in 2022?

4.1: Entrance fee each hue royal tomb in 2022

To get tickets for the tombs and monuments in Hue, you can buy at the ticket boxes in front of each tomb or at the Citadel.

The entrance fee seems so high if you buy the ticket for each tomb separately. Therefore, Combo tickets are sold to save money for the tourists who are planning to visit several attractions of the complex of Hue Monuments. The combo tickets are more beneficial.

Most of the royal tombs in Hue are easy to reach by car, motorbike and bicycle.

title: How To Get from Hue To Hoi An
description: The Best Way to Travel from Hue to Hoi An 1: Which sightseeing will you stop from Hue to Hoi An 1.1: The City of Ghost 1.2: Lap An...
content:
The Best Way to Travel from Hue to Hoi An
1: Which sightseeing will you stop from Hue to Hoi An
1.1: The City of Ghost
1.2: Lap An Lagoon
1.3: Lang Co Beach
1.4: Hai Van Pass
1.5: Marble Mountain
2: How to Get to Hoi An from Hue
2.1: By Private Car
2.2: By Limousine Bus
2.3: By Bus
2.4: By Train to Da Nang and get Taxi to Hoi An
2.5: By Motorbike

Hoi An is an exceptionally well-preserved ancient town in Central Vietnam, it’s the epitome of Vietnam’s slogan, Timeless Beauty. Hoi An Old Town has been a UNESCO recognized World Heritage Cultural Site since 1999. This well-kept South-East Asian trading port has been one of the most visited cities of the country. Hoi An surely has a long history, beautiful fusion of culture and architecture that attracts thousands of both local and international tourists daily

The distance from Hue to Hoi An is approximately 125 kilometers. Getting from Hue to Hoi An has surely been improved over the past years. It’s no longer the usual point A to point B trip. Not to miss any of the beautiful, captivating and unusual sights, tourists started adding a few stops along the way.

Which sightseeing will you stop from Hue to Hoi An
The City of Ghost

One of the unusual sights when traveling from Hue to Hoi An is the mysterious City of Ghosts. Yes, visiting tombs is no longer odd when in Hue. The imperial city of Hue does not only have the royal tombs of the Nguyen dynasty but has a luxurious cemetery as well. The City of Ghost is a proof of how locals value their dead loved ones, spending tons of money to build aesthetic graves with different magnificent designs.
The City of Ghosts is located a little outside the ancient city of Hue. An Bang is a fishing village which is about 35 kilometers away from Hue city. An Bang Cemetery became a must-see place when traveling from Hue to Hoi An by car for its unique sight. A multitude of diverse styles and ancient motifs makes the City of Ghosts, a reflection of culture, architecture and beliefs

One of the tombs in An Bang cemetery
Lap An Lagoon

The beautiful scenery at the foot of Bach Ma Mountain is what tourists will get when visiting the Lap An Lagoon. The lagoon reflects the exquisite green mountains of Bach Ma and the blue skies and clouds.
Located at the Lang Co, the town is almost midway from Hue to Hoi An Tour. A quick stop while sipping coffee or fresh coconut and immersing themselves with the fascinating sight of the 800-hectare lagoon is one way to enjoy the trip to Hoi An from Hue.

Lang Co Beach

The white sand and blue waters of Lang Co beach attracts both local and international tourists during a Hue to Hoi An tour. While in a small rustic town like Lang Co, taking a quick refreshing swim in the serene and less touristy Lang Co beach would be a cherry on top.
Lang Co town is known for its picturesque landscape, being in the middle of two bodies of water – the Lang Co beach and the Lap An Lagoon. This can be perfectly seen and captured on Hai Van Pass.

Lap An Lagoon and Lang Co Beach
Hai Van Pass

Hai Van Pass connects the two famous provinces in Central Vietnam, Da Nang and Thua Thien Hue. The 21 kilometer long mountain pass is commonly visited by daredevils traveling from Hue to Hoi An by motorbike as it was featured by Top Gear. It was one of the most dangerous passes in Vietnam before as the visibility reduces due to clouds and the mist from the sea.
The peak of Hai Van Pass rewards visitors a mesmerizing panoramic view of Danang City and Lang Co town. As beautiful as it is, Hai Van Pass is more than just a pass but has a historical significance because it served as a physical border between the Champa Kingdom and Dai Viet (old Vietnam).

Hai Van Pass
Marble Mountain

After the daring yet mesmerizing scenery in Hai Van Pass, a visit to the Marble Mountain is a must. Marble Mountains is located in the middle of the bustling city of Danang. The cluster of 5 marble and limestone mountains has an interesting story and Thuy mountain, the largest among these 5 mountains, is the most visited as it holds several Hindu and Buddhist temples and grottoes.
However, these beautiful and sacred religious shrines may not be the reason why most people visit the mountain. At the summit of Marble Mountains, the panoramic view of the famous Danang City with its awe-inspiring architecture can be seen while a marble carving village that sells masterpieces made out of stones from the mountain is at the foot of the Marble Mountains

Marble Mountain
How to Get to Hoi An from Hue
By Private Car

Probably the most convenient way to go from Hue to Hoi An By Private Car. Getting a private car to Hoi An from Hue, comes with a professional local English speaking driver who is an expert with the routes and Vietnamese behavior on the road. This is very important since the route might include the dangerous yet beautiful Hai Van Pass.

By Limousine Bus

When traveling to Hoi An from Hue on a budget but do not want the experience of getting on a cramped and mostly overloaded bus, tourists can take a limousine bus instead. Hoi An from Hue by limousine bus is one of the newest and budget-friendly ways to travel.

By Bus

WProbably by far, the cheapest way to get to Hoi An from Hue. A trip from Hue to Hoi An by bus is the most budget-friendly and culturally immersing experience as most locals would travel this way. However, getting to Hoi An from Hue by bus could be a little inconvenient especially when traveling with family or with several luggages.

By Train to Da Nang and get Taxi to Hoi An

As there is no train station in Hoi An, Danang Station is the nearest train station to Hoi An. For tourists who wish to get to Hoi An after arriving in Danang by train, hailing a taxi can be a good choice. By Motorbike

One of the best ways to get to Hoi An from Hue is getting on a motorcycle. Traveling from Hue to Hoi An by motorbike would be the most exciting. Feeling the cool breeze while passing through small towns and rustic villages from Hue to Hoi An gives a different experience.

title: An Bang Cemetary – The City of Ghosts
description: About An Bang Cemetery An Bang Cemetery Location Why its called the City of Ghosts What to see in An Bang cemetery How to visit An Bang cemetery About...
content:
About An Bang Cemetery
An Bang Cemetery Location
Why its called the City of Ghosts
What to see in An Bang cemetery
How to visit An Bang cemetery
About An Bang Cemetery

An Bang Cemetary, beside the popular and famous attractions in Hue such as Imperial city, Thien Mu pagoda and royal tombs, there is a unique destination which tourists must visit which is called An Bang Cemetery.
After the Vietnam reunification in 1975, many Vietnamese people from An Bang village left their hometown to go abroad such as Western, Europe and America to find jobs in order to have a better life. After that, they sent back money to build graves for their relatives in this village. Some people also built the tombs even though the family members have not died yet.
Honoring ancestors is one of the special cultures of Vietnam and locals enjoy building many mausoleums around here.
An Bang has become the largest cemetery in Vietnam with affluent tombs. Usually, typical figures are between twenty five to fifty thousand dollars to build a tomb. This magnificent place has become one of the tourist attractions for exploring.

One of the tombs in An Bang cemetery
An Bang Cemetery Location

It is located in the fishing village of An Bang, Thua Thien – Hue province, which is 30 km east of Hue city. It stretches along the side of the road for 3 kilometers, and there are thousands of tombs with different architectural shapes

Why its called the City of Ghosts

An Bang cemetery is considered as the most luxurious tombs in Vietnam. There are thousands of colossal, colored tombs of all shapes. It is quite scary to visit this place as it is in a quiet village near Thuan An beach and surrounded by a lot of graves. Therefore, local people call it the city of ghosts

Local people are doing worship in front of the grave
What to see in An Bang cemetery

Coming to An Bang cemetery, you will visit thousands of lavish and elaborate tombs which are especially built by mosaic architecture. Some of the mausoleums are like the mansion. This is also an opportunity to learn about the local customs of graves in Vietnam.
Factually, tombs in An Bang mausoleum city have various styles: Buddhism, Taoism, Catholicism, Vietnamese, Chinese and Western & hellip. Notably, many tombs in An Bang are the duplicates of Nguyen King’s graves and the Hue Royal Citadel. Many distinctive traditional crafts in Vietnam are restored thanks to this City of Ghosts. For instance, making dragons, phoenixes by bits of glazed terra cotta and potteries on walls, etc.

Suggest Book Private Car to Seeing: Hue Private Cars

Another mausoleum in An Bang cemetery
How to visit An Bang cemetery

From Hue city center, there are two ways you can visit this cemetery, you can either choose to travel here by motorbike or by private car. It will take approximately 60 minutes to reach the destination
If you would like to go to Hoi An. You want to visit An Bang Cemetary on the way, You can get a trip from Hue to Hoi An By Car.
